goal: Understand how to use am/is/are.
rule: Use am with I, is with he/she/it, and are with you/we/they.

examples.english: I am a designer.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi là một nhà thiết kế.

examples.english: They are students.
examples.vietnamese: Họ là học sinh.

practice.question: She ___ my friend.

goal: Talk about habits and daily routines.
rule: Use subject + base verb. Add s/es for he, she, it.

examples.english: I go to work every day.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đi làm mỗi ngày.

practice.question: He ___ coffee every morning.

goal: Talk about completed actions in the past.
rule: Use verb-ed or irregular verbs.

examples.english: I visited my parents yesterday.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã thăm bố mẹ hôm qua.

goal: Talk about actions happening right now.
rule: Use subject + am/is/are + verb-ing.

examples.english: I am writing a line of code.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đang viết một dòng mã.

examples.english: They are watching a movie.
examples.vietnamese: Họ đang xem một bộ phim.

practice.question: Listen! The birds ___ in the garden.

goal: Express ability or inability.
rule: Use subject + can/cannot (can't) + base verb.

examples.english: She can speak English fluently.
examples.vietnamese: Cô ấy có thể nói tiếng Anh trôi chảy.

examples.english: I can't swim in deep water.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi không biết bơi ở chỗ nước sâu.

practice.question: He is a chef, so he ___ cook amazing food.

goal: Show ownership or possession.
rule: Use my, your, his, her, its, our, their before a noun.

examples.english: This is my new laptop.
examples.vietnamese: Đây là máy tính xách tay mới của tôi.

examples.english: Where is her office?
examples.vietnamese: Văn phòng của cô ấy ở đâu?

practice.question: We love ___ new apartment near the city center.

goal: Talk about the existence of things.
rule: Use 'There is' with singular nouns and 'There are' with plural nouns.

examples.english: There is a mirror on the wall.
examples.vietnamese: Có một cái gương ở trên tường.

examples.english: There are thirty students in the class.
examples.vietnamese: Có ba mươi học sinh ở trong lớp.

practice.question: ___ any fresh milk left in the fridge?

goal: Give orders, instructions, or advice.
rule: Use the base verb directly without a subject. Add 'Don't' for negative.

examples.english: Open the guidebook on page 10.
examples.vietnamese: Hãy mở sách hướng dẫn ở trang 10.

examples.english: Don't walk on the grass.
examples.vietnamese: Đừng đi bộ trên cỏ.

practice.question: Please ___ quiet during the meeting.

goal: Use articles correctly with nouns.
rule: Use 'an' before vowel sounds (a, e, i, o, u), 'a' before consonants, and 'the' for specific items.

examples.english: I need to buy an umbrella.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi cần mua một cây dù.

examples.english: The coffee shop next door is closed.
examples.vietnamese: Cửa hàng cà phê bên cạnh đóng cửa rồi.

practice.question: He works for ___ international software company.

goal: Make predictions or instant decisions about the future.
rule: Use subject + will + base verb. Short form is 'll.

examples.english: I think it will rain tonight.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi nghĩ đêm nay trời sẽ mưa.

examples.english: Wait, I will open the door for you.
examples.vietnamese: Đợi đã, tôi sẽ mở cửa cho bạn.

practice.question: I'm tired. I think I ___ go to bed early tonight.

goal: Talk about plans and intentions already decided.
rule: Use subject + am/is/are + going to + base verb.

examples.english: We are going to fly to Da Nang this summer.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi dự định sẽ bay đi Đà Nẵng vào mùa hè này.

practice.question: She ___ start her own online fashion business next month.

goal: Compare two people, things, or places.
rule: Add -er to short adjectives. Use 'more' before long adjectives. Always use 'than'.

examples.english: Ho Chi Minh City is larger than Hanoi.
examples.vietnamese: Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh thì lớn hơn Hà Nội.

examples.english: This leather bag is more expensive than that one.
examples.vietnamese: Cái túi da này đắt hơn cái túi kia.

practice.question: The subway is usually ___ than a regular bus.

goal: Compare three or more things to find the top one.
rule: Use 'the' + adjective-est for short adjectives. Use 'the most' for long adjectives.

examples.english: He is the tallest player in our basketball club.
examples.vietnamese: Cậu ấy là cầu thủ cao nhất trong câu lạc bộ bóng rổ của chúng tôi.

examples.english: This is the most difficult lesson in the textbook.
examples.vietnamese: Đây là bài học khó nhất trong cuốn sách giáo khoa.

practice.question: Landmark 81 is one of the ___ buildings in Southeast Asia.

goal: Talk about life experiences up to now.
rule: Use subject + have/has + past participle (V3/ed). Use 'ever' or 'never' commonly.

examples.english: I have visited that war remnants museum twice.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã đến thăm bảo tàng chứng tích chiến tranh đó hai lần rồi.

examples.english: She has never eaten traditional Vietnamese food before.
examples.vietnamese: Cô ấy chưa bao giờ ăn món ăn truyền thống Việt Nam trước đây.

practice.question: Have you ___ seen a dolphin in the sea?

goal: Give or ask for advice and opinions.
rule: Use subject + should/should not (shouldn't) + base verb.

examples.english: You should save money for the future.
examples.vietnamese: Bạn nên tiết kiệm tiền cho tương lai.

examples.english: We shouldn't spend too much time watching TV.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta không nên dành quá nhiều thời gian để xem TV.

practice.question: You feel nervous. I think you ___ take a brief moment to relax.

goal: Use pronouns as the object of a verb.
rule: Use me, you, him, her, it, us, them after a verb or a preposition.

examples.english: The shop assistant is always polite to them.
examples.vietnamese: Nhân viên cửa hàng luôn lịch sự với họ.

examples.english: Can you help me with this project?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có thể giúp tôi dự án này không?

practice.question: I love this song. Please don't turn ___ off.

topic: Prepositions of Time: In / On / At
goal: Talk about when an event happens.
rule: Use in for months or years, on for days or dates, and at for specific times.

examples.english: The history class starts at exactly 8 AM.
examples.vietnamese: Lớp học lịch sử bắt đầu vào đúng 8 giờ sáng.

examples.english: Are you coming to my birthday party on Friday?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có đến dự tiệc sinh nhật của tôi vào thứ Sáu không?

practice.question: The leaves turn beautiful shades of yellow ___ autumn.

topic: Prepositions of Place: In / On / At
goal: Describe where something is located.
rule: Use in for enclosed spaces or cities, on for surfaces, and at for specific locations.

examples.english: There is a mirror on the wall.
examples.vietnamese: Có một cái gương ở trên tường.

examples.english: Our head office is located in the city center.
examples.vietnamese: Văn phòng chính của chúng tôi nằm ở trung tâm thành phố.

practice.question: The factory workers are demanding safer working conditions ___ the factory.

goal: Talk about indefinite quantities.
rule: Use some in positive sentences and any in negative sentences or questions.

examples.english: There is some fresh milk in the fridge.
examples.vietnamese: Có một ít sữa tươi trong tủ lạnh.

examples.english: I can't find any car keys anywhere.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi không tìm thấy bất kỳ chiếc chìa khóa xe nào ở đâu cả.

practice.question: Do you have ___ time to grab a coffee before work?

topic: Demonstrative Pronouns: This / That / These / Those
goal: Point out specific people or things.
rule: Use this/these for near objects and that/those for far objects.

examples.english: This is the most difficult lesson in the textbook.
examples.vietnamese: Đây là bài học khó nhất trong cuốn sách giáo khoa.

examples.english: Those cows are eating green grass in the field over there.
examples.vietnamese: Những con bò đằng kia đang ăn cỏ xanh trên đồng.

practice.question: ___ shoes in my hand are made of durable rubber.

goal: Ask for specific information using question words.
rule: Use Who for people, What for things, Where for places, and When for time.

examples.english: Where is your office located?
examples.vietnamese: Văn phòng của bạn nằm ở đâu?

examples.english: What is your exact height and weight?
examples.vietnamese: Chiều cao và cân nặng chính xác của bạn là bao nhiêu?

practice.question: ___ is the head chef preparing for our party?

goal: Talk about actions that were in progress at a specific time in the past.
rule: Use subject + was/were + verb-ing.

examples.english: We were browsing the local art gallery all afternoon.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi đã dành cả buổi chiều để xem triển lãm nghệ thuật địa phương.

practice.question: The little boy ___ with a remote-controlled car when I arrived.

goal: Describe how an action is performed.
rule: Add -ly to most adjectives. Place the adverb after the main verb or object.

examples.english: She can speak English fluently.
examples.vietnamese: Cô ấy có thể nói tiếng Anh một cách trôi chảy.

examples.english: The audience clapped loudly at the end of the show.
examples.vietnamese: Khán giả đã vỗ tay lớn khi kết thúc buổi biểu diễn.

practice.question: Modern technology has ___ changed traditional agriculture.

goal: Express strong obligation or prohibition.
rule: Use subject + must/must not + base verb to show rules or laws.

examples.english: Students must wear white shirts as part of the school uniform.
examples.vietnamese: Học sinh phải mặc áo sơ mi trắng như một phần của đồng phục trường.

examples.english: You mustn't accept gifts or candy from a stranger.
examples.vietnamese: Cháu không được phép nhận quà hay kẹo từ người lạ.

practice.question: Vehicles ___ stop when the traffic light turns red.

goal: Explain the reason why someone does something.
rule: Use to + base verb to mean in order to.

examples.english: He is trying to save money to buy a new laptop.
examples.vietnamese: Cậu ấy đang cố gắng tiết kiệm tiền để mua một chiếc máy tính xách tay mới.

examples.english: He washed his face with cold water to wake up.
examples.vietnamese: Cậu ấy đã rửa mặt bằng nước lạnh để tỉnh táo.

practice.question: She stays slim by exercising and eating clean every day ___ her health.

goal: Use verb-ing correctly after certain verbs.
rule: Use the gerund form (verb-ing) after verbs like like, love, hate, enjoy, or spend time.

examples.english: I absolutely love listening to live acoustic music.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi hoàn toàn yêu thích việc nghe nhạc acoustic sống.

examples.english: We shouldn't spend too much time watching TV.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta không nên dành quá nhiều thời gian để xem TV.

practice.question: I hate ___ in long lines at the supermarket.

goal: Talk about general truths or scientific facts.
rule: Use If/When + Present Simple, Present Simple.

examples.english: If you heat rubber, it melts.
examples.vietnamese: Nếu bạn làm nóng cao su, nó sẽ tan chảy.

practice.question: When it rains, the green grass ___ wet.

goal: Distinguish between nouns you can and cannot count.
rule: Use a/an or plural forms for countable nouns. Do not use plural forms for uncountable nouns like water, rice, or information.

examples.english: I bought three apples and some fresh milk.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã mua ba quả táo và một ít sữa tươi.

practice.question: Can you give me some ___ about the local art gallery?

goal: Ask about quantities of things.
rule: Use How many with plural countable nouns. Use How much with uncountable nouns.

examples.english: How many students are there in the class?
examples.vietnamese: Có bao nhiêu học sinh ở trong lớp học?

examples.english: How much cash do you have in your wallet?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có bao nhiêu tiền mặt trong ví?

practice.question: ___ coffee do you drink every morning?

goal: Talk about how often you do something.
rule: Place always, usually, often, sometimes, never before the main verb, but after the verb to be.

examples.english: I go to work every day. I am always on time.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đi làm mỗi ngày. Tôi luôn luôn đúng giờ.

examples.english: She often visits her parents at the weekend.
examples.vietnamese: Cô ấy thường đến thăm bố mẹ vào cuối tuần.

practice.question: He is a vegetarian, so he ___ eats meat.

goal: Connect phrases and sentences logically.
rule: Use and for addition, but for contrast, or for choices, and because for reasons.

examples.english: I wanted to buy that leather bag, but it was too expensive.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi muốn mua chiếc túi da đó, nhưng nó quá đắt.

practice.question: She stays at home ___ she feels extremely tired.

goal: Show that something belongs to a person.
rule: Add apostrophe s to a singular name or noun to show possession.

examples.english: Where is the head chef room?
examples.vietnamese: Phòng của bếp trưởng ở đâu?

examples.english: This is Mary new laptop.
examples.vietnamese: Đây là máy tính xách tay mới của Mary.

practice.question: My ___ car is parked near the city center.

goal: Use plural pronouns as objects.
rule: Use us, you, them as objects after a main verb or preposition.

examples.english: Our teacher gave us a very difficult lesson.
examples.vietnamese: Giáo viên của chúng tôi đã cho chúng tôi một bài học rất khó.

practice.question: The managers want to see the factory workers. Call ___ please.

goal: Express simple preferences.
rule: Follow the verbs like, love, and hate with a verb ending in -ing.

examples.english: They absolutely love watching romantic movies.
examples.vietnamese: Họ hoàn toàn yêu thích việc xem phim lãng mạn.

practice.question: I hate ___ early when it rains outside.

goal: Offer something or make polite requests.
rule: Use would like + noun or would like + to + base verb.

examples.english: Would you like some fresh milk or hot tea?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có muốn một ít sữa tươi hay trà nóng không?

examples.english: I would like to order a cup of black coffee.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi muốn gọi một tách cà phê đen.

practice.question: We would like ___ a mirror for our bedroom wall.

goal: Describe movement towards a place.
rule: Use prepositions like to, into, or through to show movement or direction.

examples.english: The little boy ran into the local art gallery.
examples.vietnamese: Cậu bé chạy vào trong triển lãm nghệ thuật địa phương.

practice.question: The subway train goes ___ a long dark tunnel.

goal: Differentiate singular and plural nearby items.
rule: Use this for one nearby item and these for multiple nearby items.

examples.english: This line of code solves our major system error.
examples.vietnamese: Dòng mã này giải quyết lỗi hệ thống nghiêm trọng của chúng ta.

practice.question: ___ documents on my desk need to be signed immediately.

goal: Master past forms that do not end in -ed.
rule: Memorize unique past forms for common irregular verbs like go to went, buy to bought, see to saw.

examples.english: We went to Da Nang last summer and saw beautiful beaches.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi đã đi Đà Nẵng mùa hè năm ngoái và thấy những bãi biển đẹp.

practice.question: He ___ a durable rubber raincoat yesterday afternoon.

goal: Talk about real or possible future situations.
rule: Use If + Present Simple, will + base verb.

examples.english: If the weather is fine tomorrow, we will visit the war remnants museum.
examples.vietnamese: Nếu ngày mai thời tiết đẹp, chúng tôi sẽ đi thăm bảo tàng chứng tích chiến tranh.

practice.question: If you don't save money now, you ___ be able to buy a new laptop.

topic: Present Perfect with For and Since
goal: Talk about situations that started in the past and continue now.
rule: Use since for a specific starting point in time and for for a duration of time.

examples.english: Our head office has been in Ho Chi Minh City since 2018.
examples.vietnamese: Văn phòng chính của chúng tôi đã ở Thành phố Hồ Chí Minh từ năm 2018.

examples.english: He has worked as a designer for five years.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã làm việc như một nhà thiết kế được năm năm rồi.

practice.question: The factory workers have been on strike ___ two weeks.

goal: Focus on the action rather than the person doing it.
rule: Use subject + am/is/are + past participle (V3/ed).

examples.english: English is spoken fluently by many young people here.
examples.vietnamese: Tiếng Anh được nói trôi chảy bởi rất nhiều người trẻ ở đây.

examples.english: These durable shoes are made of rubber.
examples.vietnamese: Những đôi giày bền này được làm từ cao xu.

practice.question: Millions of emails ___ by corporate employees every day.

goal: Express weak possibility about the present or future.
rule: Use subject + may/might + base verb to show that something is possible but not certain.

examples.english: We might fly to Da Nang if we get cheap tickets.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi có thể sẽ bay đi Đà Nẵng nếu săn được vé rẻ.

practice.question: Take an umbrella. It ___ rain heavily later tonight.

goal: Talk about general necessity and lack of obligation.
rule: Use have to/has to for external rules. Use don't/doesn't have to when something is optional.

examples.english: You don't have to dress formally for the staff party.
examples.vietnamese: Bạn không cần phải ăn mặc quá trang trọng cho bữa tiệc nhân viên đâu.

practice.question: He ___ wake up early tomorrow because it is Sunday.

goal: Describe degree and sufficiency.
rule: Put too before adjectives. Put enough after adjectives but before nouns.

examples.english: This leather bag is too expensive for me to buy.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc túi da này quá đắt để tôi có thể mua.

examples.english: He is experienced enough to lead the software team.
examples.vietnamese: Cậu ấy đủ kinh nghiệm để dẫn dắt đội ngũ phần mềm.

practice.question: I don't have ___ money to purchase that premium laptop.

goal: Add extra information to a sentence cleanly.
rule: Use who for people and which for things or animals.

examples.english: The head chef who designed this unique menu is very famous.
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng người mà thiết kế thực đơn độc đáo này rất nổi tiếng.

examples.english: I need a software tool which can check system errors quickly.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi cần một công cụ phần mềm cái mà có thể kiểm tra lỗi hệ thống nhanh chóng.

practice.question: This is the guidebook ___ provides information about local art galleries.

goal: Talk about past habits or states that are no longer true.
rule: Use subject + used to + base verb. Use didn't use to for negatives.

examples.english: I used to drink a lot of coffee, but now I prefer green tea.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi từng uống rất nhiều cà phê, nhưng bây giờ tôi thích trà xanh hơn.

practice.question: She ___ live in Hanoi before moving to Ho Chi Minh City.

topic: Present Perfect with Just / Already / Yet
goal: Talk about recently completed actions.
rule: Use just and already in positive sentences before the V3. Use yet at the end of negative sentences or questions.

examples.english: The software engineer has already fixed that major line of code.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm đã sửa xong dòng mã nghiêm trọng đó rồi.

examples.english: Have you visited the war remnants museum yet?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn đã đi thăm bảo tàng chứng tích chiến tranh chưa?

practice.question: I am not hungry. I have ___ eaten a bowl of traditional beef noodles.

goal: Place adjectives correctly in a sentence.
rule: Put adjectives before nouns or after the verb to be.

examples.english: She bought a beautiful leather bag.
examples.vietnamese: Cô ấy đã mua một chiếc túi da đẹp.

examples.english: This software tool is useful.
examples.vietnamese: Công cụ phần mềm này rất hữu ích.

practice.question: They work in a ___ office near the river.

topic: Prepositions of Place: Under / Next to / Between
goal: Describe precise locations of objects.
rule: Use under for directly below, next to for beside, and between for in the middle of two things.

examples.english: The coffee shop is right next to the local art gallery.
examples.vietnamese: Quán cà phê nằm ngay cạnh triển lãm nghệ thuật địa phương.

practice.question: The cat is sleeping ___ the kitchen table.

goal: Identify singular object pronouns in context.
rule: Replace singular nouns with me, you, him, her, it when they receive the action.

examples.english: The head chef called her into the kitchen.
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng đã gọi cô ấy vào trong bếp.

practice.question: I saw the software engineer yesterday, but I didn't speak to ___.

goal: Form negative sentences in the present simple.
rule: Use do not (don't) with I/you/we/they and does not (doesn't) with he/she/it + base verb.

examples.english: He doesn't eat meat because he is a vegetarian.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy không ăn thịt vì anh ấy là một người ăn chay.

practice.question: We ___ have enough money to buy that premium laptop.

goal: Ask questions in the present simple tense.
rule: Use Do or Does at the beginning of the sentence before the subject.

examples.english: Do you go to work by subway every day?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có đi làm bằng tàu điện ngầm mỗi ngày không?

practice.question: ___ she wear a uniform at her new office?

goal: Ask open questions using Wh- words and helping verbs.
rule: Structure: Wh- word + do/does + subject + base verb.

examples.english: Where does the head chef buy these fresh ingredients?
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng mua những nguyên liệu tươi ngon này ở đâu?

practice.question: What time ___ the history class start tomorrow?

goal: Use adjectives correctly after linking verbs.
rule: Use adjectives (not adverbs) after verbs like feel, look, smell, taste, sound.

examples.english: The traditional beef noodles taste delicious.
examples.vietnamese: Món phở bò truyền thống có vị rất ngon.

practice.question: You look ___ before the exam. Take a moment to relax.

goal: Form irregular plural nouns correctly.
rule: Some nouns have irregular plural forms like man to men, child to children, person to people.

examples.english: There are many people waiting in line at the supermarket.
examples.vietnamese: Có rất nhiều người đang đứng đợi thành hàng ở siêu thị.

practice.question: The ___ are playing with a remote-controlled car in the garden.

goal: Use size and shape descriptors correctly.
rule: Place size descriptors before shape descriptors when modifying a noun.

examples.english: There is a large round mirror on the wall.
examples.vietnamese: Có một cái gương tròn lớn ở trên tường.

practice.question: The factory workers gathered inside a ___ tent.

goal: Use prefer to state a choice over another option.
rule: Use subject + prefer + noun or verb-ing to show general preference.

examples.english: I prefer green tea to black coffee.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi thích trà xanh hơn cà phê đen.

practice.question: We prefer ___ to live acoustic music rather than loud rock.

goal: Form negative sentences about past events.
rule: Use subject + did not (didn't) + base verb for all subjects.

examples.english: We didn't visit the war remnants museum yesterday.
examples.vietnamese: Hôm qua chúng tôi đã không đi thăm bảo tàng chứng tích chiến tranh.

practice.question: He ___ bring his raincoat, so he got completely wet.

goal: Ask questions about completed past actions.
rule: Structure: Did + subject + base verb?

examples.english: Did you fly to Da Nang last summer?
examples.vietnamese: Mùa hè năm ngoái bạn có bay đi Đà Nẵng không?

practice.question: ___ the software engineer solve that system error?

goal: Understand relative pronouns acting as subjects or objects.
rule: Who or which can be followed directly by a verb (subject clause) or by a subject + verb (object clause).

examples.english: This is the guidebook which I bought at the shop.
examples.vietnamese: Đây là cuốn sách hướng dẫn cái mà tôi đã mua ở cửa hàng.

practice.question: The designer ___ we hired yesterday is very creative.

goal: Express general ability or inability in the past.
rule: Use subject + could/could not (couldn't) + base verb.

examples.english: When he was a child, he could run very fast.
examples.vietnamese: Khi còn là một đứa trẻ, cậu ấy có thể chạy rất nhanh.

practice.question: I ___ read the document because it was written in a foreign language.

goal: Compare things that are equal or unequal.
rule: Use as + adjective + as. Use not as... as to show one is less than the other.

examples.english: The regular bus is not as fast as the subway.
examples.vietnamese: Xe buýt thông thường thì không nhanh bằng tàu điện ngầm.

practice.question: This new smartphone is as ___ as my laptop.

goal: Use reflexive pronouns when the subject and object are the same.
rule: Use myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves.

examples.english: The head chef prepared this unique dish himself.
examples.vietnamese: Đích thân bếp trưởng đã tự tay chuẩn bị món ăn độc đáo này.

practice.question: The software engine updates ___ automatically every midnight.

goal: Talk about past events where the focus is on the object.
rule: Use subject + was/were + past participle (V3/ed).

examples.english: This durable rubber raincoat was made in Vietnam.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc áo mưa cao su bền bỉ này đã được sản xuất tại Việt Nam.

practice.question: The beautiful art gallery ___ opened by a famous designer in 2022.

goal: Choose between to-infinitive and -ing after specific verbs.
rule: Verbs like decide, plan, want take to-infinitive. Verbs like enjoy, mind, avoid take gerund (-ing).

examples.english: We decided to fly to Da Nang for our summer vacation.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi đã quyết định bay đi Đà Nẵng cho kỳ nghỉ hè.

practice.question: The software engineer finished ___ the line of code.

topic: Quantifiers: A lot of / Much / Many
goal: Express large quantities correctly.
rule: Use a lot of in positive sentences. Use many for countable and much for uncountable in negative sentences or questions.

examples.english: There are a lot of cows eating grass in the field.
examples.vietnamese: Có rất nhiều con bò đang ăn cỏ trên đồng.

examples.english: I don't have much time to visit the museum today.
examples.vietnamese: Hôm nay tôi không có nhiều thời gian để đi thăm bảo tàng.

practice.question: How ___ factory workers are there in this facility?

topic: Time Clauses: When / As soon as
goal: Connect two actions happening at specific times.
rule: Use when or as soon as to introduce a time clause. Use present simple when referring to future intentions.

examples.english: As soon as I arrive at the office, I will send you the document.
examples.vietnamese: Ngay khi tôi đến văn phòng, tôi sẽ gửi tài liệu cho bạn.

practice.question: When the rain ___, we will walk to the local coffee shop.

goal: Replace possessive adjectives + nouns to avoid repetition.
rule: Use mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs. Never place a noun directly after them.

examples.english: This premium laptop is mine, not yours.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp này là của tôi, không phải của bạn.

practice.question: I found a leather bag. Is it ___?

topic: Prepositions of Place: In front of / Behind
goal: Describe relative positions of two objects.
rule: Use in front of for the forward part and behind for the back part of something.

examples.english: My brother's car is parked right in front of the office.
examples.vietnamese: Xe ô tô của anh trai tôi đang đỗ ngay trước văn phòng.

practice.question: The warehouse is located ___ the main factory building.

goal: Use simple descriptors for people and items.
rule: Place appearance adjectives directly before nouns to describe physical traits.

examples.english: The tall chef is preparing a unique menu.
examples.vietnamese: Người đầu bếp cao lớn đang chuẩn bị một thực đơn độc đáo.

practice.question: She wears a ___ uniform to work every single day.

goal: Strengthen the meaning of adjectives or adverbs.
rule: Place very or really directly before adjectives or other adverbs.

examples.english: The history class this morning was very interesting.
examples.vietnamese: Lớp học lịch sử sáng nay rất thú vị.

practice.question: We are ___ nervous about the final software system test.

goal: Make quick, friendly group suggestions.
rule: Use Let's + base verb. It is a short form for Let us.

examples.english: Let's grab a cup of black coffee before the meeting.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta hãy đi uống một tách cà phê đen trước cuộc họp đi.

practice.question: ___ browse the local art gallery this weekend!

goal: Ask for agreement on a suggestion politely.
rule: Use Shall we + base verb at the beginning of a question.

examples.english: Shall we walk to the supermarket together?
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta cùng đi bộ đến siêu thị nhé?

practice.question: ___ visit the war remnants museum tomorrow morning?

goal: Talk about small quantities.
rule: Use a little with uncountable nouns. Use a few with plural countable nouns.

examples.english: There are a few cows resting in the field.
examples.vietnamese: Có một vài con bò đang nghỉ ngơi trên đồng cỏ.

practice.question: I only need ___ fresh milk for my hot tea.

goal: Describe a change in state or condition.
rule: Follow become or get with an adjective to show transition.

examples.english: The weather gets cold when it rains heavily.
examples.vietnamese: Thời tiết trở nên lạnh khi trời mưa lớn.

practice.question: The software engineer became ___ after working for ten hours.

goal: Avoid repeating a noun that was mentioned before.
rule: Use one to replace a singular noun and ones to replace a plural noun.

examples.english: I don't like this guidebook, show me that one.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi không thích cuốn sách hướng dẫn này, hãy đưa tôi xem cuốn kia.

practice.question: Which shoes do you prefer? The rubber ___ or the leather ones?

goal: Use the present continuous for confirmed future arrangements.
rule: Structure: subject + am/is/are + verb-ing accompanied by a future time word.

examples.english: We are flying to Da Nang next Monday.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi sẽ bay đi Đà Nẵng vào thứ Hai tới.

practice.question: The head chef ___ a unique dish for the audience tonight.

goal: Talk about imaginary or impossible situations in the present.
rule: Use If + Past Simple, would + base verb. Use were for all subjects with the verb to be.

examples.english: If I were you, I would purchase that durable rubber raincoat.
examples.vietnamese: Nếu tôi là bạn, tôi sẽ mua chiếc áo mưa cao su bền bỉ đó.

practice.question: If he had enough cash, he ___ buy a premium laptop today.

goal: Show which past action happened before another past action.
rule: Use subject + had + past participle (V3/ed).

examples.english: When the audience arrived, the acoustic music show had already started.
examples.vietnamese: Khi khán giả đến nơi, buổi biểu diễn nhạc acoustic đã bắt đầu rồi.

practice.question: The software engineer ___ fixed the system error before the office opened.

goal: Report what someone said by shifting the tense backward.
rule: Change present simple to past simple, and change am/is/are to was/were.

examples.english: He said that he worked as a designer at a famous office.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy nói rằng anh ấy làm việc như một nhà thiết kế tại một văn phòng nổi tiếng.

practice.question: Mary told me that she ___ very nervous about the project.

topic: Modal Verbs of Deduction: Must / Can't
goal: Make strong logical guesses about a situation.
rule: Use must when you are certain something is true. Use can't when you are certain it is impossible.

examples.english: He works all day at the factory. He must be extremely tired.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy làm việc cả ngày ở nhà máy. Chắc chắn anh ấy phải rất mệt.

practice.question: She has a brand new luxury vehicle. She ___ be poor.

goal: Show possession or ownership within a relative clause.
rule: Use whose to replace possessive words like his, her, their followed by a noun.

examples.english: I met a factory worker whose brother works at our office.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã gặp một công nhân nhà máy người mà có anh trai làm việc tại văn phòng chúng tôi.

practice.question: This is the famous chef ___ unique recipes are known worldwide.

goal: Check information or ask for agreement using short tags.
rule: Use a negative tag for positive sentences and a positive tag for negative sentences.

examples.english: You live near the local art gallery, don't you?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn sống gần triển lãm nghệ thuật địa phương, phải không?

practice.question: He doesn't drink black coffee, ___ he?

goal: Form question tags for past tense actions.
rule: Use did or didn't followed by the subject pronoun at the end of the sentence.

examples.english: They flew to Da Nang last summer, didn't they?
examples.vietnamese: Họ đã bay đi Đà Nẵng mùa hè năm ngoái, phải không?

practice.question: The software engineer didn't find any major line of code errors, ___ he?

goal: Emphasize qualities and descriptions.
rule: Use so directly before adjectives or adverbs. Use such before an adjective + noun combination.

examples.english: This leather bag is so expensive that I cannot purchase it.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc túi da này đắt đến mức tôi không thể mua nó.

practice.question: It was ___ a difficult textbook lesson that nobody could pass the quiz.

goal: Connect two options or choices clearly.
rule: Use either... or for positive choices. Use neither... nor to reject both options.

examples.english: Neither the regular bus nor the subway goes directly to that museum.
examples.vietnamese: Cả xe buýt thông thường lẫn tàu điện ngầm đều không đi thẳng đến bảo tàng đó.

practice.question: You can have ___ fresh milk or hot tea, choose one.

goal: Express regrets or desires about a current situation.
rule: Use subject + wish + subject + Past Simple tense.

examples.english: I wish I could see a wild dolphin in the sea right now.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi ước gì mình có thể nhìn thấy một chú cá heo hoang dã trên biển ngay bây giờ.

practice.question: The designer wishes he ___ in a larger modern office.

goal: Give direct orders, warnings, or instructions.
rule: Use the base verb directly without a subject at the beginning of the sentence.

examples.english: Open the guidebook on page 20 to see the map.
examples.vietnamese: Hãy mở cuốn sách hướng dẫn ở trang 20 để xem bản đồ.

practice.question: ___ walk on the green grass inside the local art gallery.

goal: Describe things that are higher than an object.
rule: Use over or above when something is at a higher level than another object.

examples.english: Those cows are eating grass under the bridge over the river.
examples.vietnamese: Những con bò đó đang ăn cỏ dưới cây cầu bắc qua sông.

practice.question: The helicopter flew directly ___ the main factory building.

goal: Ask for or give permission in an informal way.
rule: Use can + subject + base verb for questions, and subject + can/cannot for statements.

examples.english: Can I use your premium laptop for a brief moment?
examples.vietnamese: Tôi có thể sử dụng chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp của bạn một lát được không?

practice.question: You ___ park your vehicle here; it is a restricted zone.

goal: Use 'it' correctly as a singular object pronoun for things.
rule: Place 'it' after a verb or preposition to replace a singular noun that is not a person.

examples.english: I like this leather bag. I want to buy it.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi thích chiếc túi da này. Tôi muốn mua nó.

practice.question: Where is my raincoat? I can't find ___ anywhere.

goal: Talk about total quantities or whole groups.
rule: Use all with plural nouns and every with singular countable nouns.

examples.english: Every student must wear a uniform on Mondays.
examples.vietnamese: Mỗi học sinh đều phải mặc đồng phục vào các ngày thứ Hai.

practice.question: ___ the factory workers are demanding safer working conditions.

goal: Combine habits with specific time markers.
rule: Place phrases like every morning, twice a week, or on weekends at the end of the sentence.

examples.english: I drink a cup of black coffee every morning.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi uống một tách cà phê đen mỗi buổi sáng.

practice.question: She visits her parents ___ a month.

goal: Show the sequence of events in time.
rule: Use before to indicate an earlier time and after to indicate a later time.

examples.english: He always washes his face with cold water before work.
examples.vietnamese: Cậu ấy luôn rửa mặt bằng nước lạnh trước khi đi làm.

practice.question: The audience clapped loudly ___ the acoustic music show ended.

goal: Show possession for plural groups.
rule: Place our or their directly before a noun to show ownership by multiple people.

examples.english: Our head office is located in the city center.
examples.vietnamese: Văn phòng chính của chúng tôi nằm ở trung tâm thành phố.

practice.question: The cows are eating ___ green grass in the field.

goal: Describe standard emotional states.
rule: Follow linking verbs or the verb to be with an emotion adjective.

examples.english: The little boy feels happy because he got a toy car.
examples.vietnamese: Cậu bé cảm thấy hạnh phúc vì có được một chiếc ô tô đồ chơi.

practice.question: The designer felt ___ when the system error deleted his files.

goal: Express zero quantity of something.
rule: Use no followed directly by a noun. Use none as a pronoun on its own.

examples.english: I have no cash left in my wallet.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi không còn chút tiền mặt nào trong ví cả.

practice.question: How many books do you have? ___, I left them at the office.

goal: Combine two nouns to create a new specific concept.
rule: The first noun acts like an adjective to describe the second main noun.

examples.english: Let's meet at the local coffee shop.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta hãy gặp nhau ở quán cà phê địa phương nhé.

practice.question: The history ___ starts at exactly 8 AM tomorrow.

goal: Connect a cause to its direct result.
rule: Use 'so' to link a reason clause to a result clause.

examples.english: It was raining heavily, so I brought a durable rubber raincoat.
examples.vietnamese: Trời đang mưa to, vì vậy tôi đã mang theo một chiếc áo mưa cao su bền bỉ.

practice.question: He is a vegetarian, ___ he does not order any meat dishes.

goal: Point to multiple objects at a distance.
rule: Use 'those' for plural objects that are far away from the speaker.

examples.english: Those cows over there are eating green grass.
examples.vietnamese: Những con bò đằng kia đang ăn cỏ xanh.

practice.question: ___ buildings across the river belong to a software corporate.

goal: Ask about actions happening right at the moment of speaking.
rule: Structure: Am/Is/Are + subject + verb-ing?

examples.english: Is the head chef preparing our party dinner right now?
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng có đang chuẩn bị bữa tối cho bữa tiệc của chúng ta ngay lúc này không?

practice.question: ___ you browsing the local art gallery online?

goal: Confirm proper alignment of to be with basic subjects.
rule: Use am with I, is with he/she/it, and are with you/we/they.

examples.english: This line of code is the solution to our system error.
examples.vietnamese: Dòng mã này là giải pháp cho lỗi hệ thống của chúng ta.

practice.question: The software tools ___ ready for download.

goal: Describe an ongoing past action interrupted by a sudden event.
rule: Use Past Continuous for the longer action, and Past Simple after 'when' for the short action.

examples.english: The little boy was playing with a toy car when I arrived.
examples.vietnamese: Cậu bé đang chơi với một chiếc ô tô đồ chơi thì tôi đến.

practice.question: We ___ the local art gallery when the power went out.

goal: Talk about life experiences up to now.
rule: Use 'ever' in questions and 'never' in negative statements before the V3 form.

examples.english: Have you ever seen a wild dolphin in the sea?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn đã bao giờ nhìn thấy cá heo hoang dã trên biển chưa?

practice.question: The designer has ___ visited that specific war remnants museum.

goal: Reinforce structure of the passive voice for completed events.
rule: Structure: subject + was/were + past participle (V3/ed).

examples.english: This premium laptop was purchased by my brother last month.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp này đã được anh trai tôi mua vào tháng trước.

practice.question: These important documents ___ signed by the manager yesterday morning.

goal: Give advice or state an opinion on what is best.
rule: Use subject + should/should not + base verb.

examples.english: We shouldn't spend too much time watching TV.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta không nên dành quá nhiều thời gian xem tivi.

practice.question: You feel nervous. You ___ take a brief moment to relax.

goal: Use a verb ending in -ing as the subject of a sentence.
rule: Treat a gerund subject as singular, following it with a singular verb form.

examples.english: Exercising every day helps she stay slim and fit.
examples.vietnamese: Tập thể dục mỗi ngày giúp cô ấy giữ được vóc dáng thon gọn.

practice.question: ___ standard textbooks can sometimes be boring for students.

goal: Talk about plans or intentions made before speaking.
rule: Structure: subject + am/is/are + going to + base verb.

examples.english: She is going to start her own online fashion business next month.
examples.vietnamese: Cô ấy dự định sẽ bắt đầu công việc kinh doanh thời trang trực tuyến của riêng mình vào tháng tới.

practice.question: We ___ fly to Da Nang for our upcoming summer vacation.

goal: Compare differences between two objects.
rule: Add -er to short adjectives. Use 'more' before long adjectives + than.

examples.english: The subway is usually faster than a regular bus.
examples.vietnamese: Tàu điện ngầm thường nhanh hơn xe buýt thông thường.

practice.question: This leather bag is ___ than that cloth one.

goal: Describe an object which has the highest degree of a quality.
rule: Use the + adjective-est for short forms. Use the most + adjective for long forms.

examples.english: This is the most difficult lesson in the textbook.
examples.vietnamese: Đây là bài học khó nhất trong cuốn sách giáo khoa.

practice.question: He is the ___ player in our basketball club.

goal: Identify and use irregular adverbs of manner correctly.
rule: Some adverbs do not end in -ly, such as good to well, fast to fast, hard to hard.

examples.english: She can speak English very well after living abroad.
examples.vietnamese: Cô ấy có thể nói tiếng Anh rất tốt sau khi sống ở nước ngoài.

practice.question: The software engineer worked ___ to fix the system error before 8 AM.

goal: Adjust personal pronouns correctly when reporting statements.
rule: Shift pronouns like I to he/she and my to his/her to match the original speaker.

examples.english: He said that his new office was near the subway station.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy nói rằng văn phòng mới của anh ấy ở gần ga tàu điện ngầm.

practice.question: Mary told me that ___ was looking for her lost raincoat.

topic: Indefinite Pronouns: Someone / Anyone / No one
goal: Refer to people without specifying who they are.
rule: Use someone in positive statements, anyone in questions/negatives, and no one for zero quantity.

examples.english: There is someone waiting at the office reception desk.
examples.vietnamese: Có ai đó đang đợi ở bàn tiếp tân của văn phòng.

practice.question: I didn't see ___ at the local art gallery this afternoon.

topic: Making Requests: Can you / Could you
goal: Ask someone to do something politely.
rule: Use Could you + base verb for a more formal or polite request than Can you.

examples.english: Could you send me the important document via email?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có thể gửi cho tôi tài liệu quan trọng qua email được không?

practice.question: ___ you help me find my leather bag, please?

goal: Use a that-clause to express opinions or facts after certain verbs.
rule: Follow verbs like think, believe, say, or know with 'that' + a full subject-verb clause.

examples.english: I think that modern technology has changed traditional agriculture completely.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi nghĩ rằng công nghệ hiện đại đã thay đổi hoàn toàn nền nông nghiệp truyền thống.

practice.question: The managers believe ___ the factory workers deserve a bonus.

goal: Link adjectives to a following action smoothly.
rule: Structure: subject + be + adjective + to-infinitive (to + base verb).

examples.english: It is normal to feel nervous before a major examination.
examples.vietnamese: Cảm thấy bối rối trước một kỳ thi lớn là chuyện bình thường.

practice.question: The chef was glad ___ that the audience loved his unique dish.

topic: Prepositions after Verbs: Listen to / Think about
goal: Use correct fixed prepositions after specific common verbs.
rule: Always match look/listen with 'to', and think/worry with 'about' before an object.

examples.english: I absolutely love listening to live acoustic music.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi hoàn toàn yêu thích việc nghe nhạc acoustic sống.

practice.question: The software engineer is thinking ___ a solution for the error.

goal: Describe whether an object is enclosed within a space or exterior to it.
rule: Use inside when an object is contained by boundaries, and outside when it is in the open air or exterior.

examples.english: The factory workers are gathered inside the main building.
examples.vietnamese: Các công nhân nhà máy đang tập trung bên trong tòa nhà chính.

practice.question: Please leave your wet umbrella ___ the office door.

goal: Show ownership or possession by one person.
rule: Add an apostrophe and an 's' ('s) to the end of a singular noun.

examples.english: My brother's new laptop is extremely fast.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay mới của anh trai tôi cực kỳ nhanh.

practice.question: The head ___ unique recipe won a global culinary award.

goal: Show ownership or possession by more than one person.
rule: Add only an apostrophe (') to regular plural nouns ending in -s. Add ('s) to irregular plurals.

examples.english: The students' textbooks are stored in the back cupboard.
examples.vietnamese: Sách giáo khoa của các học sinh được cất trong tủ phía sau.

practice.question: The factory ___ uniforms are made of heavy durable cotton.

goal: Identify and use plural object pronouns correctly.
rule: Use us, you, them when a plural noun receives the action of a verb or follows a preposition.

examples.english: The tour guide showed them the map of the local art gallery.
examples.vietnamese: Người hướng dẫn du lịch đã cho họ xem bản đồ của triển lãm nghệ thuật địa phương.

practice.question: We are lost. Can you help ___ find the nearest subway station?

goal: Express what someone is able or unable to do in the present.
rule: Use subject + can/cannot (can't) + base verb for all subjects.

examples.english: That software engineer can write code very quickly.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm đó có thể viết mã rất nhanh.

practice.question: He is a strict vegetarian, so he ___ eat this beef noodle dish.

goal: Tell someone not to do something directly.
rule: Structure: Do not (Don't) + base verb at the beginning of the sentence.

examples.english: Don't touch that line of code; it will break the entire system.
examples.vietnamese: Đừng chạm vào dòng mã đó; nó sẽ làm hỏng toàn bộ hệ thống đấy.

practice.question: ___ drop your cash inside the crowded subway carriage.

goal: Express purpose, destination, or recipient using 'for'.
rule: Follow 'for' with a noun or pronoun to indicate who receives something or its target purpose.

examples.english: I bought this beautiful leather bag for my mother.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã mua chiếc túi da đẹp này cho mẹ tôi.

practice.question: This guidebook is essential ___ tourists visiting Da Nang.

goal: Talk about inclusion or exclusion of items or people.
rule: Use with to mean accompanied by or containing. Use without to mean lacking.

examples.english: He prefers a cup of black coffee without any sugar.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy thích một tách cà phê đen không có chút đường nào.

practice.question: You shouldn't walk out into the rain ___ an umbrella.

goal: Give a direct reason for an action or situation.
rule: Use 'because' to connect a main clause to a subordinate clause that explains the cause.

examples.english: We took the subway because the regular bus was delayed.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi đi tàu điện ngầm vì xe buýt thông thường bị trễ.

practice.question: She feels nervous ___ she has a major presentation tomorrow.

goal: Describe habits at extreme ends of the frequency scale.
rule: Place always and never before main verbs, but after the verb to be.

examples.english: The head chef never uses frozen ingredients for his dishes.
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng không bao giờ sử dụng nguyên liệu đông lạnh cho các món ăn của mình.

practice.question: He is ___ on time for the morning history class.

goal: Describe normal or periodic habits.
rule: Place often and sometimes before the main verb. Sometimes can also go at the beginning of a sentence.

examples.english: We often listen to live acoustic music on weekends.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi thường nghe nhạc acoustic sống vào cuối tuần.

practice.question: The software engineer ___ works late to fix unexpected errors.

goal: Talk about indefinite quantities in general statements.
rule: Use some in positive sentences. Use any in negative sentences and general questions.

examples.english: Are there any fresh apples left in the kitchen?
examples.vietnamese: Còn quả táo tươi nào ở trong bếp không?

practice.question: I need to buy ___ durable rubber boots at the supermarket.

topic: Prepositions of Place: At the office / At home
goal: Target standard institutional or residential locations.
rule: Use 'at' when referring to a place in terms of its function or as a specific point.

examples.english: The designer is currently working at the office.
examples.vietnamese: Nhà thiết kế hiện tại đang làm việc tại văn phòng.

practice.question: I left my important documents ___ home this morning.

topic: Prepositions of Place: In a room / In a building
goal: Express being inside a three-dimensional container or space.
rule: Use 'in' for spaces with physical boundaries like rooms, factories, or cities.

examples.english: There are thirty students studying in the classroom.
examples.vietnamese: Có ba mươi học sinh đang học ở trong phòng học.

practice.question: The machines operate continuously ___ that large factory.

topic: Basic Quantifiers: Much vs Many review
goal: Review noun compatibility with much and many.
rule: Pair 'many' with plural countable nouns. Pair 'much' with uncountable nouns.

examples.english: How many leather bags did she purchase?
examples.vietnamese: Cô ấy đã mua bao nhiêu chiếc túi da vậy?

practice.question: There isn't ___ cash inside my wallet right now.

goal: Distinguish time markers for duration versus specific starting points.
rule: Use 'since' before a specific point in time. Use 'for' before a length or period of time.

examples.english: He has worked as a software engineer here since 2021.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã làm việc như một kỹ sư phần mềm ở đây từ năm 2021.

examples.english: They have lived in Da Nang for ten years.
examples.vietnamese: Họ đã sống ở Đà Nẵng được mười năm rồi.

practice.question: The head chef has operated this restaurant ___ five months.

goal: Form conditional sentences meaning 'if... not'.
rule: Use 'unless' followed by a positive verb form to mean 'except if'.

examples.english: Unless you bring a raincoat, you will get wet in this heavy rain.
examples.vietnamese: Trừ khi bạn mang theo áo mưa, không thì bạn sẽ bị ướt trong cơn mưa to này đấy.

practice.question: We will miss the subway ___ we leave the office immediately.

topic: Modals of Obligation: Must vs Have to
goal: Differentiate personal obligation from external rules.
rule: Use 'must' for strong personal obligation felt by the speaker. Use 'have to' for rules or external necessity.

examples.english: Factory workers have to wear protective uniforms according to safety laws.
examples.vietnamese: Công nhân nhà máy phải mặc đồng phục bảo hộ theo luật an toàn.

practice.question: I ___ fix this critical system error tonight; I promised the manager.

goal: Express that an action is strictly forbidden.
rule: Structure: subject + must not (mustn't) + base verb. It means it is against the law or rules.

examples.english: Visitors mustn't touch the items inside the art gallery.
examples.vietnamese: Khách tham quan không được phép chạm vào các hiện vật bên trong triển lãm nghệ thuật.

practice.question: You ___ take photos inside this military museum; it's illegal.

topic: Lack of Obligation: Don't have to
goal: Express that an action is optional or unnecessary.
rule: Use subject + do/does not have to + base verb. It means you can do it if you want, but it's not required.

examples.english: Tomorrow is Sunday, so the software engineer doesn't have to go to the office.
examples.vietnamese: Ngày mai là Chủ Nhật, vì vậy kỹ sư phần mềm không phải đến văn phòng.

practice.question: We have plenty of cash, so you ___ find an ATM right now.

goal: Provide essential details about a place using 'where'.
rule: Use 'where' to connect a relative clause to a noun representing a location or building.

examples.english: This is the quiet coffee shop where I study English textbook lessons.
examples.vietnamese: Đây là quán cà phê yên tĩnh nơi mà tôi học các bài trong sách giáo khoa tiếng Anh.

practice.question: The factory ___ they manufacture durable rubber tires is huge.

goal: Provide essential details about a time period or date using 'when'.
rule: Use 'when' to connect a relative clause to a noun representing a time, day, or year.

examples.english: Mondays are the days when all students must wear their uniforms.
examples.vietnamese: Thứ Hai là những ngày mà tất cả học sinh đều phải mặc đồng phục.

practice.question: 2022 was the year ___ the designer opened his modern art gallery.

goal: Indicate excessive amount or sufficiency regarding a quality.
rule: Place 'too' directly before an adjective. Place 'enough' directly after an adjective.

examples.english: This leather bag is too expensive for a regular student to buy.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc túi da này quá đắt đối với một học sinh bình thường để có thể mua.

practice.question: He didn't run fast ___ to catch the early subway train.

goal: Express sufficiency regarding a quantity of items.
rule: Place 'enough' directly before a countable plural noun or an uncountable noun.

examples.english: Do we have enough cash to pay for the traditional beef noodles?
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta có đủ tiền mặt để trả cho món phở bò truyền thống không?

practice.question: There aren't enough ___ for all the students in the history class.

goal: Talk about past habits or states that are no longer true today.
rule: Structure: subject + used to + base verb for all personal subjects.

examples.english: He used to eat meat, but now he is a strict vegetarian.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã từng ăn thịt, nhưng bây giờ anh ấy là một người ăn chay trường.

practice.question: We used to ___ to live acoustic music at that old coffee shop.

goal: Form negative statements or ask questions about past habits.
rule: Use 'didn't use to' for negatives and 'Did + subject + use to...?' for questions. Drop the 'd' from 'used'.

examples.english: Did you use to visit the war remnants museum when you lived here?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có từng thường đi thăm bảo tàng chứng tích chiến tranh khi còn sống ở đây không?

practice.question: I didn't ___ like history textbook lessons when I was a child.

goal: Express a temporary action happening around the present time but not permanently.
rule: Structure: subject + am/is/are + verb-ing combined with words like 'this week' or 'these days'.

examples.english: The software engineer is working from home this week because his office is being painted.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm đang làm việc tại nhà tuần này vì văn phòng của anh ấy đang được sơn.

practice.question: The factory workers ___ extra hours these days to finish the order.

goal: Ensure proper singular verb matching with indefinite pronouns.
rule: Always treat words like everyone, someone, somebody, no one as singular subjects.

examples.english: Everyone loves listening to live acoustic music at the local gallery.
examples.vietnamese: Mọi người đều yêu thích nghe nhạc acoustic sống tại triển lãm địa phương.

practice.question: Someone ___ left a beautiful leather bag inside the subway carriage.

goal: Apply the -ing form of a verb immediately after a preposition.
rule: Whenever a verb follows a preposition (like in, on, at, about, for, before, after), it must be a gerund (-ing).

examples.english: He apologized for making an unexpected error in the line of code.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã xin lỗi vì đã tạo ra một lỗi không mong muốn trong dòng mã.

practice.question: Are you interested in ___ the war remnants museum with us?

topic: Reported Speech with Tell vs Say
goal: Differentiate reported reporting verbs based on object inclusion.
rule: Use 'say' when there is no direct personal object. Use 'tell' when followed immediately by a person (object pronoun).

examples.english: The manager told me that the factory order was ready.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý nói với tôi rằng đơn hàng của nhà máy đã sẵn sàng.

practice.question: She ___ that she wanted to buy a durable rubber raincoat.

topic: Prepositions of Place: On a surface / On public transport
goal: Describe objects on a surface or passengers inside large public vehicles.
rule: Use 'on' for flat surfaces (table, wall) and for large public transit vehicles where you can walk around (bus, train, plane).

examples.english: I left my modern smartphone on the desk at the office.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã để quên chiếc điện thoại thông minh hiện đại của mình trên bàn làm việc ở văn phòng.

practice.question: There are thirty factory workers ___ the regular bus right now.

goal: Express the result or consequence of an action.
rule: Use 'so' to connect a cause clause to a result clause. Usually preceded by a comma.

examples.english: The weather was extremely hot, so we decided to go to the beach.
examples.vietnamese: Thời tiết vô cùng nóng, vì vậy chúng tôi đã quyết định đi ra bãi biển.

practice.question: He didn't want to make a mistake, ___ he checked the line of code carefully.

goal: Describe activities that happen almost never.
rule: Place rarely and seldom before main verbs, but after auxiliary or 'be' verbs. They have a negative meaning.

examples.english: The strict manager rarely praises his software engineering team.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý nghiêm khắc hiếm khi khen ngợi đội ngũ kỹ sư phần mềm của mình.

practice.question: She is a vegetarian, so she ___ eats fresh beef noodles.

goal: Express general preferences using nouns directly.
rule: Follow verbs of preference directly with singular/plural nouns or uncountable nouns without a preposition.

examples.english: The children absolutely love live rock music.
examples.vietnamese: Lũ trẻ hoàn toàn yêu thích nhạc rock sống.

practice.question: Does your team manager like traditional ___ for lunch?

goal: Express desires for objects or actions properly.
rule: Use 'want + noun' for objects, and 'want + to + base verb' for actions.

examples.english: The head chef wants to open a new restaurant in Da Nang.
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng muốn mở một nhà hàng mới ở Đà Nẵng.

practice.question: She wants ___ a durable leather bag from the local market.

goal: Ask about the owner of a specific item.
rule: Structure: Whose + noun + is/are + pronoun/demonstrative? 'Whose' can also be followed immediately by a verb.

examples.english: Whose textbook is left inside the classroom?
examples.vietnamese: Cuốn sách giáo khoa của ai bị bỏ lại ở trong lớp học thế?

practice.question: ___ beautiful leather bag is this on the office sofa?

goal: Use this, that, these, those without nouns to identify things.
rule: Use this/that for singular items, and these/those for plural items acting directly as sentence subjects.

examples.english: These are the new safety uniforms for the factory workers.
examples.vietnamese: Đây là những bộ đồng phục bảo hộ mới dành cho công nhân nhà máy.

practice.question: ___ is my new premium laptop over there on the table.

topic: Prepositions of Time: In the morning / afternoon / evening
goal: Reference general parts of the day correctly.
rule: Always use 'in the' before morning, afternoon, and evening. Use 'at' for night.

examples.english: I usually drink a cup of hot black coffee in the morning.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi thường uống một tách cà phê đen nóng vào buổi sáng.

practice.question: The software engineers have an important team meeting ___ afternoon.

topic: Subject Pronoun vs Possessive Adjective review
goal: Ensure clarity between who performs an action and who owns an item.
rule: Subject pronouns (I, you, he) start clauses. Possessive adjectives (my, your, his) must be followed by a noun.

examples.english: He washed his face before walking to the head office.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã rửa mặt trước khi đi bộ đến văn phòng chính.

practice.question: ___ history textbook is very heavy, so I leave it at home.

goal: Make direct commands or requests polite.
rule: Add 'please' at either the beginning or the end of an imperative sentence. Use a comma if placed at the end.

examples.english: Please pay attention to the map of the local art gallery.
examples.vietnamese: Vui lòng chú ý vào bản đồ của triển lãm nghệ thuật địa phương.

practice.question: Take a photo of our development team, ___.

goal: Refer to a singular animal, object, or abstract idea as the object of a verb.
rule: Use 'it' after a transitive verb or preposition to replace a singular non-human noun phrase.

examples.english: I found a beautiful leather bag, and I bought it immediately.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã tìm thấy một chiếc túi da rất đẹp, và tôi đã mua nó ngay lập tức.

practice.question: This beef noodle dish is delicious. You must try ___.

goal: Form plural regular nouns ending in specific consonants correctly.
rule: Add '-es' instead of just '-s' to nouns ending in -ch, -sh, -x, -s, or -z.

examples.english: The head chef prepared two large boxes of fresh vegetables.
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng đã chuẩn bị hai hộp lớn rau củ tươi.

practice.question: We bought thirty durable leather ___ for our digital devices.

goal: Indicate the subject matter or topic of a conversation, book, or lesson.
rule: Follow 'about' with a noun phrase to point out the core theme or topic.

examples.english: The students are listening to a lecture about modern agriculture.
examples.vietnamese: Các học sinh đang nghe một bài giảng về nền nông nghiệp hiện đại.

practice.question: This guidebook gives detailed information ___ the historical sites in Da Nang.

topic: Adjectives: Word Order with Color and Size
goal: Sequence multiple descriptive adjectives correctly before a noun.
rule: Place size adjectives before color adjectives. Structure: Size + Color + Noun.

examples.english: The designer carried a large black leather bag into the room.
examples.vietnamese: Nhà thiết kế đã mang một chiếc túi da màu đen lớn vào trong phòng.

practice.question: He rides a ___ motorcycle to the head office every morning.

goal: Form standard short spoken forms of negative to-be sentences.
rule: Use isn't (is not) and aren't (are not). Note that 'am not' cannot be contracted to 'amn't'.

examples.english: They aren't ready to give the presentation about the new software yet.
examples.vietnamese: Họ vẫn chưa sẵn sàng để đưa ra bài thuyết trình về phần mềm mới.

practice.question: The factory worker ___ at the building site this morning.

topic: Reported Speech with Shift: Present to Past
goal: Change direct speech statements into reported statements with a tense change.
rule: When the reporting verb is in the past (said, told), shift the present simple verbs in direct speech into the past simple.

examples.english: Direct: 'I need some cash.' -> Indirect: He said that he needed some cash.
examples.vietnamese: Trực tiếp: 'Tôi cần một ít tiền mặt.' -> Gián tiếp: Anh ấy nói rằng anh ấy cần một ít tiền mặt.

practice.question: The software engineer told me that he ___ a critical system error.

goal: Focus a sentence on the recipient of an action rather than the doer in the present.
rule: Structure: Subject + am/is/are + Past Participle (V3/V-ed). Use 'by' to mention the agent.

examples.english: Durable rubber tires are manufactured by large factory machines.
examples.vietnamese: Lốp xe cao su bền bỉ được sản xuất bởi những chiếc máy móc nhà máy lớn.

practice.question: Traditional beef noodles ___ served hot in this restaurant every day.

goal: Focus a sentence on the recipient of a past action.
rule: Structure: Subject + was/were + Past Participle (V3/V-ed).

examples.english: This beautiful leather bag was designed by a famous local artist.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc túi da đẹp đẽ này đã được thiết kế bởi một nghệ sĩ địa phương nổi tiếng.

practice.question: The system code ___ fixed by the software engineer yesterday morning.

goal: Explain why someone does an action using a shortened form.
rule: Use 'to + base verb' directly to mean 'in order to' or 'so as to'. Do not use 'for do'.

examples.english: He went to the local coffee shop to buy a hot black coffee.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã đến quán cà phê địa phương để mua một ly cà phê đen nóng.

practice.question: She studied the history textbook carefully ___ pass the final test.

goal: Show possession or ownership inside a defining relative clause.
rule: Replace a possessive determiner (his, her, their) with 'whose' followed immediately by a noun.

examples.english: I met the talented designer whose modern art gallery opened in Da Nang.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã gặp nhà thiết kế tài năng người mà có triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại vừa mở ở Đà Nẵng.

practice.question: The head chef, ___ traditional recipes are famous, won a global award.

topic: Verbs followed by Gerund or Infinitive with Meaning Change: Stop
goal: Distinguish between ending an activity versus pausing to perform a new action.
rule: Stop + gerund (-ing) means to halt the activity completely. Stop + to-infinitive means to pause what you are doing in order to do something else.

examples.english: He stopped eating meat to become a strict vegetarian.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã dừng ăn thịt hẳn để trở thành một người ăn chay trường.

examples.english: The factory workers stopped to have a short break at 3 PM.
examples.vietnamese: Các công nhân nhà máy đã dừng việc lại để nghỉ giải lao một lát vào lúc 3 giờ chiều.

practice.question: The software engineer stopped ___ to music because he needed to focus on the code.

topic: Verbs followed by Gerund or Infinitive with Meaning Change: Remember
goal: Distinguish between remembering a past action versus remembering a future obligation.
rule: Remember + gerund (-ing) refers to a memory of doing something in the past. Remember + to-infinitive means not forgetting to perform a duty.

examples.english: Please remember to lock the corporate head office door before leaving.
examples.vietnamese: Vui lòng nhớ khóa cửa văn phòng chính của tập đoàn trước khi về.

practice.question: I remember ___ this exact war remnants museum when I was ten years old.

goal: Refer to general, non-specific locations or total lack of locations.
rule: Use everywhere for all places, somewhere for an unspecified single place, and nowhere for zero places (requires a positive verb).

examples.english: We looked everywhere for my lost history textbook but couldn't find it.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi đã tìm kiếm khắp mọi nơi cuốn sách giáo khoa lịch sử bị mất của tôi nhưng không thể tìm thấy.

practice.question: There is ___ quiet to work inside this noisy factory building.

goal: Form confirmation checks at the end of sentences using do/does/did.
rule: If the main statement uses a simple present or past active verb, form the tag question using the appropriate form of 'do'. Match negative statements with positive tags and vice versa.

examples.english: The software engineer worked late last night, didn't he?
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm đã làm việc muộn tối qua, đúng không?

practice.question: Your brother doesn't eat meat, ___ he?

goal: Describe moving from one side of a three-dimensional or crowded space to the other.
rule: Use 'through' when moving inside a tunnel, forest, heavy rain, or a crowded building.

examples.english: The early subway train traveled swiftly through the dark tunnel.
examples.vietnamese: Chuyến tàu điện ngầm sớm đã di chuyển nhanh chóng xuyên qua đường hầm tối tăm.

practice.question: He had to walk ___ the heavy rain because he didn't bring an umbrella.

topic: Prepositions of Movement: Into / Out of
goal: Describe entering or exiting an enclosed space or structure.
rule: Use 'into' to show movement toward the inside of a room/building. Use 'out of' to show movement toward the exterior.

examples.english: The designer walked into the modern art gallery at exactly 9 AM.
examples.vietnamese: Nhà thiết kế đã bước vào triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại vào đúng 9 giờ sáng.

practice.question: The factory workers rushed ___ the building when the fire alarm rang.

goal: State that two entities have the exact same level of a specific quality.
rule: Structure: subject + verb + as + base adjective + as + noun/pronoun phrase.

examples.english: This premium laptop is just as expensive as my old desktop computer.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp này đắt tiền ngang ngửa chiếc máy tính để bàn cũ của tôi.

practice.question: The history test wasn't as ___ as the students expected.

topic: Comparison: Not as... as with Adverbs
goal: Express inequality in how actions are performed.
rule: Structure: subject + negative auxiliary verb + base adverb + as + noun/pronoun phrase.

examples.english: The new apprentice doesn't cook as skillfully as the head chef.
examples.vietnamese: Người học việc mới không nấu ăn khéo léo bằng vị bếp trưởng.

practice.question: I cannot write code as ___ as the senior software engineer.

topic: Quantifiers: A great deal of / A large amount of
goal: Express large quantities with uncountable concepts formally.
rule: Use 'a great deal of' or 'a large amount of' exclusively before uncountable nouns like cash, time, or progress.

examples.english: Developing this system code required a great deal of time.
examples.vietnamese: Việc phát triển mã hệ thống này đã đòi hỏi rất nhiều thời gian.

practice.question: They spent a large amount of ___ on renovating the corporate head office.

topic: Past Continuous vs Past Simple with While
goal: Show a background continuous past action during which another past event occurred.
rule: Use the past continuous (was/were + V-ing) immediately after 'while' to represent the longer background action.

examples.english: While the factory workers were operating the machines, a visitor arrived.
examples.vietnamese: Trong khi các công nhân nhà máy đang vận hành máy móc thì một vị khách đã đến.

practice.question: The system broke down while the engineer ___ the critical code.

topic: Possessive Pronouns: Mine / Yours / His / Hers
goal: Replace possessive adjectives + nouns to avoid repetition.
rule: Use mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs alone without a noun following them.

examples.english: This premium laptop is mine, and that one on the desk is yours.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp này là của tôi, và chiếc ở trên bàn kia là của bạn.

practice.question: She found a beautiful leather bag. Is it ___?

topic: Prepositions of Place: Next to / Beside
goal: Describe an object that is at the side of another object.
rule: Use 'next to' or 'beside' interchangeably to mean adjacent to or at the side of.

examples.english: The local coffee shop is located right next to the art gallery.
examples.vietnamese: Quán cà phê địa phương nằm ngay cạnh triển lãm nghệ thuật.

practice.question: He sat ___ the senior software engineer during the team meeting.

topic: Prepositions of Place: In front of
goal: Describe an object positioned further forward than another object.
rule: Always use the full three-word phrase 'in front of'. Do not omit 'of'.

examples.english: Thirty students are standing in front of the historical museum.
examples.vietnamese: Ba mươi học sinh đang đứng trước bảo tàng lịch sử.

practice.question: The manager parked his motorcycle right in ___ the factory gates.

goal: Describe an object positioned at the back of another object.
rule: Use 'behind' directly before the noun phrase. Do not add 'of'.

examples.english: The development team's head office is located behind that tall building.
examples.vietnamese: Văn phòng chính của đội ngũ phát triển nằm ở phía sau tòa nhà cao tầng đó.

practice.question: The kitchen is situated ___ the main dining area of the restaurant.

topic: Prepositions of Time: At specific clock times
goal: State the exact time an event happens.
rule: Always pair specific numerical times or expressions like 'noon' or 'midnight' with the preposition 'at'.

examples.english: The early subway train leaves exactly at 6 AM.
examples.vietnamese: Chuyến tàu điện ngầm sớm khởi hành chính xác vào lúc 6 giờ sáng.

practice.question: The head chef arrives at the restaurant ___ 8:30 AM.

topic: Prepositions of Time: On days of the week
goal: Reference days and specific calendar dates correctly.
rule: Always use the preposition 'on' before days of the week (Mondays, Sunday) and specific dates (on October 12th).

examples.english: The factory workers don't have to work on Sundays.
examples.vietnamese: Công nhân nhà máy không phải làm việc vào các ngày Chủ Nhật.

practice.question: We have a major presentation about the system code ___ Tuesday.

topic: Prepositions of Time: In months, years, and seasons
goal: Reference wider, longer blocks of time correctly.
rule: Use the preposition 'in' before months, years, centuries, and seasons.

examples.english: We plan to travel to Da Nang in August.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi dự định đi du lịch Đà Nẵng vào tháng Tám.

practice.question: The designer opened his modern art gallery ___ 2023.

goal: Express general hobbies or activities you enjoy or dislike.
rule: Follow verbs of preference with a gerund (verb-ing) to describe the activity as a concept.

examples.english: The software engineer hates writing long system documentation.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm ghét việc viết tài liệu hệ thống dài dòng.

practice.question: She absolutely loves ___ to live acoustic music at night.

topic: Adjectives: Comparative Form with short adjectives
goal: Compare two items using single-syllable qualities.
rule: Add '-er' to the end of short adjectives and follow with the word 'than'.

examples.english: The subway is much faster than the regular bus.
examples.vietnamese: Tàu điện ngầm nhanh hơn nhiều so với xe buýt thông thường.

practice.question: This new history textbook is ___ than the old one.

topic: Adjectives: Comparative Form with long adjectives
goal: Compare two items using multi-syllable qualities.
rule: Place 'more' before adjectives with two or more syllables, and follow with 'than'.

examples.english: A premium laptop is more expensive than a basic desktop.
examples.vietnamese: Một chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp thì đắt tiền hơn một chiếc máy tính để bàn cơ bản.

practice.question: This leather bag is ___ beautiful than that rubber pouch.

goal: Compare entities using the qualities good and bad properly.
rule: The comparative form of 'good' is 'better'. The comparative form of 'bad' is 'worse'. Never say 'gooder' or 'badder'.

examples.english: Traditional beef noodles taste better when served extremely hot.
examples.vietnamese: Phở bò truyền thống ngon hơn khi được phục vụ cực kỳ nóng.

practice.question: Making a critical system error is ___ than making a typo.

goal: Propose a joint activity to another person politely.
rule: Structure: Let's (Let us) + base verb directly without 'to'.

examples.english: Let's take a quick photo in front of the local art gallery.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta hãy chụp một bức ảnh nhanh ở trước triển lãm nghệ thuật địa phương đi.

practice.question: ___ have a hot cup of black coffee before the team meeting.

goal: Ask for agreement on a proposed action or plan.
rule: Structure: Shall we + base verb...? Used primarily in questions.

examples.english: Shall we buy some fresh vegetables for the head chef?
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta có nên mua một ít rau củ tươi cho bếp trưởng không?

practice.question: ___ we visit the historical war remnants museum this weekend?

goal: Offer an alternative idea or suggestion casually.
rule: Structure: How about + verb-ing...? Always follow 'about' with a gerund or noun phrase.

examples.english: How about going shopping for a durable leather bag tomorrow?
examples.vietnamese: Hay là ngày mai đi mua sắm một chiếc túi da bền bỉ nhỉ?

practice.question: How about ___ the subway train instead of driving through heavy traffic?

topic: Adjectives: Superlative Form with short adjectives
goal: Identify the top extreme entity among three or more items.
rule: Structure: the + adjective-er / -est. For short adjectives, add '-est' to the end.

examples.english: This is the fastest system code our software engineer has ever written.
examples.vietnamese: Đây là mã hệ thống nhanh nhất mà kỹ sư phần mềm của chúng tôi từng viết.

practice.question: Who is the ___ worker in this large factory building?

topic: Past Continuous vs Past Simple with When
goal: Show a past event that interrupted a continuous background action.
rule: Use past simple (V2/V-ed) immediately after 'when' to express the sudden interrupting event.

examples.english: The designer was drawing a leather bag pattern when the lights went out.
examples.vietnamese: Nhà thiết kế đang vẽ mẫu túi da thì đèn bỗng nhiên tắt phụt.

practice.question: I was studying the history textbook when the phone ___.

goal: Express imaginary past situations and their hypothetical outcomes.
rule: Structure: If + subject + past perfect (had + V3), subject + would have + past participle (V3).

examples.english: If the software engineer had checked the line of code, the system wouldn't have crashed.
examples.vietnamese: Nếu kỹ sư phần mềm kiểm tra dòng mã đó, hệ thống đã không bị sập.

practice.question: If we had taken the subway train, we ___ missed the presentation.

topic: Wish + Past Simple for Present Desires
goal: Express regret or a desire for a current situation to be different.
rule: Use 'wish' followed by a clause in the past simple tense to represent an unreal present desire. Use 'were' for all subjects with the verb 'be'.

examples.english: I wish I had enough cash to purchase that luxury leather bag.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi ước tôi có đủ tiền mặt để mua chiếc túi da xa xỉ đó.

practice.question: The factory worker wishes he ___ to work on Sundays.

goal: Join two related items or qualities to emphasize inclusion.
rule: Structure: both + noun/adjective + and + noun/adjective. Plural verbs are required if joining two subjects.

examples.english: Both the head chef and the restaurant manager were extremely satisfied.
examples.vietnamese: Cả bếp trưởng và người quản lý nhà hàng đều vô cùng hài lòng.

practice.question: The new boots are both durable ___ waterproof.

goal: Join two negative options to show that both are false or excluded.
