rule: Structure: neither + option A + nor + option B. The verb agrees with the subject closest to it.

examples.english: Neither the students nor the history teacher wanted to postpone the test.
examples.vietnamese: Cả các học sinh lẫn giáo viên lịch sử đều không muốn hoãn bài kiểm tra.

practice.question: Neither the subway ___ the bus goes directly to the art gallery.

goal: Present a choice between two clear alternatives.
rule: Structure: either + choice A + or + choice B. The verb matches the final subject component.

examples.english: You can have either traditional beef noodles or fresh vegetarian soup.
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có thể ăn phở bò truyền thống hoặc súp chay tươi.

practice.question: We can go to the local office either by subway ___ by taxi.

goal: Express strong certainty about a present situation based on facts.
rule: Use 'must + base verb' when you are 90-100% sure that something is true.

examples.english: He has worked for twelve hours; he must be extremely exhausted.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã làm việc suốt mười hai tiếng đồng hồ; chắc chắn anh ấy phải kiệt sức lắm.

practice.question: The designer owns a premium laptop and an art gallery; he ___ rich.

goal: Express strong certainty that a present situation is impossible.
rule: Use 'can't + base verb' when you are certain something is false based on clear logic.

examples.english: She is a strict vegetarian, so this beef noodle bowl can't be hers.
examples.vietnamese: Cô ấy là một người ăn chay trường, nên tô phở bò này chắc chắn không thể là của cô ấy được.

practice.question: The office doors are locked and dark; the manager ___ inside.

goal: Express weak possibility or uncertainty about an event.
rule: Use subject + may/might + base verb. 'Might' often implies a slightly lower probability than 'may'.

examples.english: Bring a durable rubber raincoat; it might rain heavily in Da Nang this afternoon.
examples.vietnamese: Hãy mang theo một chiếc áo mưa cao su bền bỉ; chiều nay trời có thể mưa to ở Đà Nẵng đấy.

practice.question: The system code contains an unexpected error, so it ___ break down.

topic: So vs Such with Noun Phrases
goal: Emphasize qualities inside a complete noun structure properly.
rule: Use 'so + adjective' directly. Use 'such + (a/an) + adjective + noun'.

examples.english: It was such a beautiful leather bag that she bought it instantly.
examples.vietnamese: Đó quả là một chiếc túi da đẹp đến nỗi cô ấy đã mua nó ngay lập tức.

practice.question: The software engineer wrote ___ efficient code that everyone was amazed.

goal: Indicate that the person performing the action is also the recipient.
rule: Match pronouns to the subject: I -> myself, you -> yourself (singular) / yourselves (plural).

examples.english: I fixed that unexpected system error in the code all by myself.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã tự mình sửa lỗi hệ thống không mong muốn đó trong mã.

practice.question: Did you design this entire modern art gallery layout ___?

goal: Indicate singular third-person self-directed actions.
rule: Match pronouns to the singular subjects: he -> himself, she -> herself, it -> itself.

examples.english: The head chef prepared the rare beef noodle ingredients himself.
examples.vietnamese: Chính vị bếp trưởng đã tự tay chuẩn bị các nguyên liệu phở bò quý hiếm.

practice.question: The software system automatically updates ___ every midnight.

goal: Focus a sentence on the recipient of a future action.
rule: Structure: Subject + will be + Past Participle (V3/V-ed).

examples.english: A new modern art gallery will be opened in Da Nang next year.
examples.vietnamese: Một triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại mới sẽ được khai trương tại Đà Nẵng vào năm tới.

practice.question: The factory workers' uniform designs ___ changed next month.

goal: Form passive sentences containing basic modal verbs.
rule: Structure: Subject + modal verb (can/must/should) + be + Past Participle (V3/V-ed).

examples.english: This critical line of code must be fixed immediately by the engineer.
examples.vietnamese: Dòng mã quan trọng này phải được sửa ngay lập tức bởi kỹ sư.

practice.question: Durable rubber raincoats can ___ purchased at the local supermarket.

goal: Add extra, non-essential information about an object or concept.
rule: Separate the relative clause with commas. Use 'which' (never 'that') to refer to non-human nouns.

examples.english: My new premium laptop, which cost a lot of cash, works perfectly.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp mới của tôi, cái mà tốn rất nhiều tiền mặt, hoạt động vô cùng hoàn hảo.

practice.question: The war remnants museum, ___ we visited yesterday, was highly educational.

topic: Adjectives: Superlative Form with long adjectives
goal: Identify the top extreme entity among three or more items using multi-syllable qualities.
rule: Structure: the + most + long adjective. Do not add '-est' to the end of the adjective.

examples.english: This is the most expensive leather bag in the local market.
examples.vietnamese: Đây là chiếc túi da đắt tiền nhất trong chợ địa phương.

practice.question: Writing system code is the ___ difficult task for the apprentice.

goal: Express the absolute highest or lowest quality among entities properly.
rule: The superlative form of 'good' is 'the best'. The superlative form of 'bad' is 'the worst'.

examples.english: The head chef serves the best traditional beef noodles in Da Nang.
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng phục vụ món phở bò truyền thống ngon nhất ở Đà Nẵng.

practice.question: Sabbing the main server was the ___ mistake the engineer ever made.

goal: Describe an object that is directly below or covered by another object.
rule: Use 'under' when an object is vertically below something else, often with space between them or touching.

examples.english: The office cat is sleeping soundly under the desk.
examples.vietnamese: Con mèo văn phòng đang ngủ ngon lành dưới bàn làm việc.

practice.question: He hid his spare cash ___ a heavy history textbook.

topic: Prepositions of Place: Above / Over basic
goal: Describe an object positioned higher than another object without direct contact.
rule: Use 'above' or 'over' to indicate a higher position. 'Over' often implies a path of movement or direct vertical alignment.

examples.english: There is a beautiful modern painting hanging above the sofa.
examples.vietnamese: Có một bức tranh hiện đại đẹp đẽ treo phía trên ghế sofa.

practice.question: The regular bus drove ___ the old bridge near the factory.

goal: Describe how an action is performed using regular adverbs.
rule: Form regular adverbs of manner by adding '-ly' to the descriptive adjective.

examples.english: The software engineer types code very quickly.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm gõ mã rất nhanh.

practice.question: The factory workers carried the fragile glass boxes ___.

topic: Irregular Adverbs of Manner: Fast / Hard / Well
goal: Describe actions properly using non-standard adverbs of manner.
rule: The adverb for 'good' is 'well'. The adjectives 'fast' and 'hard' remain unchanged when acting as adverbs.

examples.english: The development team worked extremely hard to finish the app.
examples.vietnamese: Đội ngũ phát triển đã làm việc vô cùng chăm chỉ để hoàn thiện ứng dụng.

practice.question: The apprentice cooks quite ___, according to the head chef.

topic: Be going to: Positive Future Statements
goal: Express future plans, intentions, or clear predictions based on evidence.
rule: Structure: subject + am/is/are + going to + base verb.

examples.english: We are going to visit the modern art gallery tomorrow afternoon.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi dự định sẽ đi thăm triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại vào chiều mai.

practice.question: The designer ___ purchase some durable leather material next week.

topic: Be going to: Negatives and Questions
goal: Form negative statements or ask questions about future intentions.
rule: Negatives: add 'not' after the to-be verb. Questions: place am/is/are before the subject.

examples.english: Are you going to take the subway train to the head office?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có định đi tàu điện ngầm đến văn phòng chính không?

practice.question: They ___ going to fix the system code tonight.

goal: Review common irregular plural nouns that do not take '-s'.
rule: Memorize individual changes: man -> men, woman -> women, child -> children, person -> people.

examples.english: There are ten people waiting outside the historical museum.
examples.vietnamese: Có mười người đang đợi bên ngoài bảo tàng lịch sử.

practice.question: The school children love listening ___ live acoustic music.

topic: Quantifiers: A lot of / Lots of basic
goal: Express large quantities with both countable and uncountable elements casually.
rule: Use 'a lot of' or 'lots of' interchangeably in positive sentences with plural or mass nouns.

examples.english: He spent a lot of cash on his premium laptop.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã chi rất nhiều tiền mặt vào chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp của mình.

practice.question: There are lots of ___ inside the new corporate building.

goal: Sequence actions or chronological steps in a paragraph clearly.
rule: Use 'First' for step one, 'Then' or 'Next' for intermediate events, and 'Finally' for the ultimate step. Usually followed by commas.

examples.english: First, the engineer wrote the code. Then, he tested it for system errors.
examples.vietnamese: Đầu tiên, kỹ sư viết mã. Sau đó, anh ấy kiểm tra xem có lỗi hệ thống không.

practice.question: ..., the head chef plated the beef noodles and sent it to the manager.

goal: Differentiate positions involving two separate entities versus groups.
rule: Use 'between' for distinct individual points (usually two). Use 'among' when surrounded by a mass or crowd.

examples.english: The leather bag fell between the desk and the wall.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc túi da rơi vào giữa bàn làm việc và bức tường.

practice.question: The designer walked ___ the crowded factory workers.

topic: Wh- Questions: Which one / Which ones
goal: Ask someone to choose between limited options clearly.
rule: Use 'Which one' for a singular choice, and 'Which ones' for plural choices.

examples.english: There are thirty textbooks on the shelf. Which one is yours?
examples.vietnamese: Có ba mươi cuốn sách giáo khoa trên kệ. Cuốn nào là của bạn?

practice.question: I see five beautiful leather bags here. Which ___ do you like?

goal: Substitute a plural non-human or human noun phrase acting as a verb object.
rule: Place 'them' directly after transitive verbs or prepositions to represent plural nouns.

examples.english: I bought some fresh vegetables and washed them carefully.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã mua một ít rau củ tươi và rửa chúng thật cẩn thận.

practice.question: The students are making mistakes. Please help ___.

topic: Time Expressions: Last week / Last year
goal: Anchor past simple sentences to specific completed timelines.
rule: Place 'last week', 'last month', or 'last year' at the absolute beginning or end of a past tense sentence. Do not add 'in' or 'on'.

examples.english: We traveled to Da Nang last year to see the modern art gallery.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi đã đi du lịch Đà Nẵng năm ngoái để xem triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại.

practice.question: The software engineer fixed the critical system error ___ week.

goal: Add extra descriptive comments about a person using non-defining relative clauses.
rule: Enclose the extra clause in commas. Use 'who' to refer to human subjects. The sentence remains grammatically complete if the clause is removed.

examples.english: The head chef, who studied culinary arts in France, loves traditional beef noodles.
examples.vietnamese: Vị bếp trưởng, người từng học nghệ thuật ẩm thực ở Pháp, rất yêu thích món phở bò truyền thống.

practice.question: My manager, ___ is usually extremely strict, smiled at the presentation.

goal: Report open questions without using direct quotation marks.
rule: Keep the Wh- word (who, what, where, when). Shift the verb tense backward and use normal statement word order (Subject + Verb). Do not use 'do/does/did'.

examples.english: Direct: 'Where is my leather bag?' -> Indirect: She asked where her leather bag was.
examples.vietnamese: Trực tiếp: 'Chiếc túi da của tôi ở đâu?' -> Gián tiếp: Cô ấy đã hỏi chiếc túi da của cô ấy ở đâu.

practice.question: The manager asked the engineer when he ___ fix the line of code.

goal: Report closed questions accurately using conditional linkers.
rule: Introduce the reported clause with 'if' or 'whether'. Use statement word order and shift the verb tense backward.

examples.english: Direct: 'Did you finish the code?' -> Indirect: The manager asked if I had finished the code.
examples.vietnamese: Trực tiếp: 'Bạn đã hoàn thành mã chưa?' -> Gián tiếp: Người quản lý đã hỏi liệu tôi đã hoàn thành mã chưa.

practice.question: The tourist asked ___ the subway train went directly to the art gallery.

topic: Causative Form: Have / Get something done
goal: Express that you arrange for someone else to perform a service or task for you.
rule: Structure: Subject + have/get + object + Past Participle (V3/V-ed).

examples.english: The company manager is going to have the office painted this weekend.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý công ty dự định sẽ cho sơn lại văn phòng vào cuối tuần này.

practice.question: I need to get my durable leather bag ___ at the local shop.

topic: Causative Form: Make someone do something
goal: Express forcing or compelling someone to execute an action.
rule: Structure: Subject + make + person + base verb (bare infinitive). Do not use 'to'.

examples.english: The strict history teacher made the thirty students rewrite their assignments.
examples.vietnamese: Giáo viên lịch sử nghiêm khắc đã bắt ba mươi học sinh viết lại bài tập của họ.

practice.question: The critical error made the software engineer ___ late into the night.

topic: Causative Form: Let someone do something
goal: Express giving permission to someone to perform an action.
rule: Structure: Subject + let + person + base verb (bare infinitive).

examples.english: The factory owner let the tired workers take a brief break.
examples.vietnamese: Chủ nhà máy đã cho phép các công nhân mệt mỏi nghỉ giải lao một lát.

practice.question: Please let me ___ a photo of this beautiful modern painting.

topic: Causative Form: Help someone do / to do something
goal: Express assisting someone with an activity.
rule: Structure: Subject + help + person + base verb OR to-infinitive. Both forms are acceptable.

examples.english: Using a premium laptop helps the designer create artwork faster.
examples.vietnamese: Sử dụng một chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp giúp nhà thiết kế tạo ra tác phẩm nghệ thuật nhanh hơn.

practice.question: Can you help me ___ this heavy leather bag onto the subway carriage?

goal: Show that an action was completed before another event in the past.
rule: Structure: Subject + had + Past Participle (V3/V-ed). Used in combination with the past simple for the later event.

examples.english: When the manager arrived at the office, the software engineer had already fixed the system code.
examples.vietnamese: Khi người quản lý đến văn phòng, kỹ sư phần mềm đã sửa xong mã hệ thống rồi.

practice.question: The regular bus ___ left before the students reached the transit station.

goal: Express regret or criticism about a past action that did not happen.
rule: Structure: Subject + should have + Past Participle (V3/V-ed). It means it was a good idea, but it wasn't done.

examples.english: You should have brought a durable rubber raincoat; it is pouring heavily.
examples.vietnamese: Bạn đáng lẽ ra nên mang theo một chiếc áo mưa cao su bền bỉ; trời đang mưa như trút nước kìa.

practice.question: The designer ___ have checked the map before driving to Da Nang.

goal: Express past ability or opportunity that was not realized.
rule: Structure: Subject + could have + Past Participle (V3/V-ed). It means the ability existed, but the action didn't happen.

examples.english: We could have taken the early subway train, but we chose to take a taxi instead.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi đã có thể đi chuyến tàu điện ngầm sớm, nhưng thay vào đó chúng tôi đã chọn đi taxi.

practice.question: The engineer could have ___ the system error easily, but he was too exhausted.

goal: Express an action that will be in progress at a specific time in the future.
rule: Structure: Subject + will be + verb-ing combined with specific future time markers.

examples.english: At exactly 10 AM tomorrow, the thirty students will be taking their history test.
examples.vietnamese: Vào đúng 10 giờ sáng mai, ba mươi học sinh sẽ đang làm bài kiểm tra lịch sử.

practice.question: This time next week, we will ___ relaxing on the beach in Da Nang.

goal: Use a clause starting with 'that' as the object of mental verbs like think, believe, know.
rule: Structure: Subject + verb (think/believe/know) + that + subject + verb. 'That' can often be omitted in casual speech.

examples.english: The head chef knows that fresh ingredients are essential for great food.
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng biết rằng nguyên liệu tươi ngon là điều thiết yếu để có món ăn tuyệt vời.

practice.question: The software engineer believes ___ the new line of code is completely error-free.

goal: Express the purpose or reason for an action using a conjunction clause.
rule: Connect the main clause to a purpose clause using 'so that' followed by a subject + modal verb (can/could/will/would).

examples.english: He arrived early so that he could secure a front seat for the presentation.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã đến sớm để anh ấy có thể giữ được một chỗ ngồi phía trước cho bài thuyết trình.

practice.question: She kept her cash inside a leather bag so ___ she wouldn't lose it.

goal: Distinguish between adjectives describing feelings versus those describing things causing the feeling.
rule: Use adjectives ending in '-ed' to describe a person's emotions. Use adjectives ending in '-ing' to describe the thing or situation causing that emotion.

examples.english: The long history lecture was very boring, so the students felt bored.
examples.vietnamese: Bài giảng lịch sử dài dòng rất nhàm chán, vì vậy các học sinh cảm thấy chán nản.

practice.question: The software development project is highly ___, making the team feel excited.

goal: Express contrast or concession linking to a noun phrase.
rule: Follow 'in spite of' or 'despite' directly with a noun, pronoun, or gerund (-ing). Do not follow directly with a subject-verb clause unless using 'the fact that'.

examples.english: Despite the heavy rain, the factory workers still arrived at the office on time.
examples.vietnamese: Mặc dù trời mưa to, công nhân nhà máy vẫn đến văn phòng đúng giờ.

practice.question: In spite of ___ a strict vegetarian, she cooked great beef noodles for her family.

goal: Anchor future sentences to specific upcoming timelines.
rule: Place 'tomorrow', 'next week', 'next month', or 'next year' at the beginning or end of future-tensed sentences.

examples.english: The software engineer will test the new line of code tomorrow.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm sẽ kiểm tra dòng mã mới vào ngày mai.

practice.question: We are going to buy a durable leather bag ___ week.

goal: Describe an action showing entry into a closed space or container.
rule: Use 'into' directly after a verb of motion (walk, put, run, drive) to show movement towards the inside of something.

examples.english: The development team walked into the main conference room for the presentation.
examples.vietnamese: Đội ngũ phát triển đã bước vào phòng hội nghị chính để thuyết trình.

practice.question: He poured the hot black coffee ___ a clean ceramic cup.

goal: Describe an action showing exit or removal from an enclosed space.
rule: Use 'out of' as a two-word preposition directly following verbs of motion.

examples.english: The factory workers rushed out of the building when the bell rang.
examples.vietnamese: Các công nhân nhà máy đã lao ra khỏi tòa nhà khi tiếng chuông vang lên.

practice.question: She carefully took her premium laptop ___ her leather bag.

goal: Describe movement in the specific direction of someone or something.
rule: Use 'towards' (or 'toward') to indicate the direction of movement, without necessarily implying reaching the destination.

examples.english: The thirty students are walking slowly towards the historical museum.
examples.vietnamese: Ba mươi học sinh đang đi bộ chậm rãi về phía bảo tàng lịch sử.

practice.question: The regular bus is heading ___ the Da Nang transit station right now.

goal: Describe movement that increases distance from a point or object.
rule: Structure: verb of motion + away from + noun phrase.

examples.english: The office cat ran away from the loud printing machine.
examples.vietnamese: Con mèo văn phòng đã chạy tránh xa khỏi chiếc máy in ồn ào.

practice.question: Please step ___ the malfunctioning factory equipment immediately.

goal: Ask for or grant permission to do an action informally.
rule: Structure: Can + subject + base verb...? Negative 'can't' is used to refuse permission.

examples.english: You can park your motorcycle right in front of the art gallery.
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có thể đỗ xe máy của mình ngay trước triển lãm nghệ thuật.

practice.question: ___ I take a quick photo of your new leather bag design?

topic: May / May not for Formal Permission
goal: Ask for or give permission in a highly professional or polite context.
rule: Structure: May + subject + base verb...? It is more formal than using 'can'.

examples.english: Students may not use their personal smartphones during the history exam.
examples.vietnamese: Học sinh không được phép sử dụng điện thoại thông minh cá nhân trong kỳ thi lịch sử.

practice.question: ___ I enter the head office to see the manager, please?

goal: Ask someone to do something for you in a polite manner.
rule: Structure: Could you + base verb...? Always keep the verb in its bare form.

examples.english: Could you please pass me that heavy history textbook?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có thể vui lòng chuyển cho tôi cuốn sách giáo khoa lịch sử dày cộp kia được không?

practice.question: Could you ___ the head chef prepare the beef noodle ingredients?

goal: Offer food, drinks, or an invitation to someone graciously.
rule: Structure: Would you like + noun phrase...? OR Would you like + to-infinitive...?

examples.english: Would you like to listen to some live acoustic music with me?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có muốn đi nghe nhạc acoustic sống cùng tôi không?

practice.question: Would you like a hot cup of black coffee before the presentation?

goal: Indicate that a quality is excessive, causing a negative result.
rule: Place 'too' directly before the adjective. It implies more than enough or more than what is desirable.

examples.english: This premium laptop is too expensive for an apprentice to buy.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp này quá đắt đối với một người thực tập để mua.

practice.question: The subway carriage was ___ crowded, so we waited for the next one.

goal: Indicate that a quality is sufficient for a specific purpose.
rule: Place 'enough' directly AFTER the descriptive adjective.

examples.english: The factory workspace is large enough for thirty new workers.
examples.vietnamese: Không gian làm việc của nhà máy đủ lớn cho ba mươi công nhân mới.

practice.question: The water in the pot isn't hot ___ to cook the traditional beef noodles.

goal: State that the quantity of an item matches the required amount.
rule: Place 'enough' directly BEFORE the countable plural noun or uncountable noun phrase.

examples.english: Do we have enough cash to purchase this durable leather bag?
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta có đủ tiền mặt để mua chiếc túi da bền bỉ này không?

practice.question: The manager doesn't have enough ___ to complete the team presentation today.

goal: Link a cause to a logical result or consequence in one sentence.
rule: Place a comma before 'so' when connecting two independent clauses.

examples.english: The system code contained a critical error, so the server crashed completely.
examples.vietnamese: Mã hệ thống chứa một lỗi nghiêm trọng, vì vậy máy chủ đã bị sập hoàn toàn.

practice.question: The designer loved Da Nang, ___ he opened a modern art gallery there.

goal: Give direct instructions, orders, or advice clearly.
rule: Start the sentence directly with the base verb. There is no stated subject pronoun.

examples.english: Wash these fresh vegetables thoroughly before giving them to the head chef.
examples.vietnamese: Hãy rửa thật sạch số rau củ tươi này trước khi đưa chúng cho bếp trưởng.

practice.question: ___ the system documentation carefully before modifying the code.

goal: Forbid an action or give negative instructions clearly.
rule: Structure: Don't (Do not) + base verb at the beginning of the sentence.

examples.english: Don't touch that hot industrial machine inside the factory.
examples.vietnamese: Đừng chạm vào chiếc máy công nghiệp đang nóng đó bên trong nhà máy.

practice.question: ___ waste your cash on low-quality leather products.

goal: Connect a surprising or contrasting fact to a main statement using a full clause.
rule: Follow 'even though' with a complete clause (Subject + Verb). It creates a stronger contrast than 'although'.

examples.english: Even though he had a premium laptop, he still preferred writing system notes by hand.
examples.vietnamese: Mặc dù anh ấy có một chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp, anh ấy vẫn thích viết ghi chú hệ thống bằng tay hơn.

practice.question: ___ the history textbook was extremely heavy, the student carried it everywhere.

goal: Indicate possession or relationship within a defining relative clause.
rule: Replace possessive adjectives (my, his, their) with 'whose' followed immediately by the noun being possessed.

examples.english: The designer met the factory worker whose leather bag pattern was stolen.
examples.vietnamese: Nhà thiết kế đã gặp người công nhân nhà máy có mẫu thiết kế túi da bị đánh cắp.

practice.question: I know the software engineer ___ system code won the tech award.

goal: Add extra non-essential possessive information into a sentence using commas.
rule: Place commas around the relative clause. Use 'whose + noun' for both people and things.

examples.english: The head chef, whose traditional beef noodles are famous, opened a new branch in Da Nang.
examples.vietnamese: Vị bếp trưởng, người có món phở bò truyền thống nổi tiếng, đã mở một chi nhánh mới tại Đà Nẵng.

practice.question: This modern art gallery, ___ owner is a famous designer, attracts many tourists.

goal: State a mandatory condition that must be met for an outcome to happen.
rule: Use 'as long as' interchangeably with 'provided that' to mean 'only if'. Followed by a present tense when referring to the future.

examples.english: You can borrow my premium laptop as long as you treat it carefully.
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có thể mượn máy tính xách tay cao cấp của tôi miễn là bạn giữ gìn nó cẩn thận.

practice.question: The manager will approve the project as long ___ the system code passes the test.

goal: Express a negative condition affecting a future potential outcome.
rule: Structure: Unless + Present Simple, Future Simple. 'Unless' means 'if... not', so do not use a negative verb inside the unless-clause.

examples.english: Unless the software engineer fixes this line of code, the system will crash.
examples.vietnamese: Trừ khi kỹ sư phần mềm sửa dòng mã này, nếu không hệ thống sẽ bị sập.

practice.question: We will miss the team presentation unless we ___ the subway train immediately.

goal: Describe regular past actions or states that no longer occur in the present.
rule: Structure: Subject + used to + base verb. Do not use this for single, one-time past events.

examples.english: The factory manager used to drive a regular bus before purchasing a luxury motorcycle.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý nhà máy từng lái xe buýt thông thường trước khi mua một chiếc xe máy sang trọng.

practice.question: She ___ study at the local art gallery every Sunday morning.

goal: State the absence of past habits or inquire about someone's former lifestyle.
rule: Negative: didn't use to (drop the 'd'). Question: Did + subject + use to (drop the 'd') + base verb...?

examples.english: The software engineer didn't use to drink black coffee when he was a student.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm đã từng không hay uống cà phê đen khi còn là một sinh viên.

practice.question: Did you ___ live in Da Nang before transferring to the head office?

goal: Express that an action or situation has become familiar or normal through experience.
rule: Structure: Subject + am/is/are + used to + verb-ing / noun phrase. Do not use a base verb.

examples.english: The veteran factory worker is used to standing for eight hours a day.
examples.vietnamese: Người công nhân nhà máy kỳ cựu đã quen với việc đứng suốt tám tiếng một ngày.

practice.question: The apprentice head chef is used to ___ under intense pressure.

goal: Describe the ongoing process of becoming familiar with a new situation.
rule: Structure: Subject + get/gets/got + used to + verb-ing / noun. It signifies the transition of adapting.

examples.english: It took the new designer months to get used to using the advanced drawing system.
examples.vietnamese: Phải mất hàng tháng trời nhà thiết kế mới quen được với việc sử dụng hệ thống vẽ nâng cao.

practice.question: You will quickly get used to ___ the subway train during rush hour.

goal: Differentiate reported speech patterns based on object requirements.
rule: Use 'say' followed directly by the reported clause (or 'to someone'). Use 'tell' followed immediately by a personal object (me, him, the manager) before the clause.

examples.english: The engineer told the manager that the line of code was fully tested.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư nói với người quản lý rằng dòng mã đã được kiểm tra đầy đủ.

practice.question: The history teacher ___ that the textbook contained thirty chapters.

goal: Report speech that carries specific speech acts or intentions smoothly.
rule: Structure: Subject + advise/remind/warn + object + to-infinitive (or not to-infinitive).

examples.english: The head chef warned the apprentice not to touch the hot beef noodle pot.
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng cảnh báo người học việc không được chạm vào nồi phở bò đang nóng.

practice.question: The manager reminded the development team ___ the code before midnight.

goal: Use an action as a conceptual subject noun in a statement.
rule: Start the sentence with a verb-ing. It always takes a singular verb form.

examples.english: Designing a high-end leather bag requires incredible artistic patience.
examples.vietnamese: Thiết kế một chiếc túi da cao cấp đòi hỏi sự kiên nhẫn nghệ thuật phi thường.

practice.question: ___ traditional beef noodles is the specialty of this local restaurant.

topic: Infinitives of Purpose (To / In order to)
goal: Explain the objective or reason behind an action using an infinitive.
rule: Use 'to + base verb' or 'in order to + base verb' to show why someone does something.

examples.english: He woke up early in order to catch the first subway train to Da Nang.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy thức dậy sớm để bắt chuyến tàu điện ngầm đầu tiên đi Đà Nẵng.

practice.question: The factory worker saved his cash ___ buy a premium laptop.

goal: Follow a preposition with a verb correctly using the gerund form.
rule: Any verb that follows a preposition (of, about, for, after, before, in, by) must be in the verb-ing form.

examples.english: The software engineer is responsible for maintaining the system code.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm chịu trách nhiệm duy trì mã hệ thống.

practice.question: After ___ thirty history textbooks, the student fell asleep at his desk.

goal: Express reactions, feelings, or judgments regarding an upcoming action.
rule: Structure: Subject + be + adjective (happy/sad/difficult/easy) + to-infinitive.

examples.english: The thirty students were happy to visit the historical museum layout.
examples.vietnamese: Ba mươi học sinh đã rất vui mừng khi được đến thăm sơ đồ bảo tàng lịch sử.

practice.question: It is very difficult ___ a complex system error without documentation.

goal: Propose a joint action or make a casual suggestion to a group.
rule: Structure: Let's + base verb. 'Let's' is a contraction of 'let us' and does not take 'to'.

examples.english: Let's eat traditional beef noodles at the local restaurant tonight.
examples.vietnamese: Tối nay chúng ta hãy đi ăn phở bò truyền thống ở nhà hàng địa phương đi.

practice.question: Let's ___ the subway train instead of driving a regular bus.

goal: Suggest a shared activity politely or ask for agreement.
rule: Structure: Shall we + base verb...? Used primarily in questions with 'we' or 'I'.

examples.english: Shall we visit the modern art gallery after the presentation?
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta sẽ đi thăm triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại sau bài thuyết trình chứ?

practice.question: Shall we ___ the software engineer to fix the system code?

goal: Propose an idea or alternative casually using a gerund or noun.
rule: Structure: How about / What about + verb-ing OR noun phrase...?

examples.english: How about buying this durable leather bag for your manager?
examples.vietnamese: Hay là mua chiếc túi da bền bỉ này tặng người quản lý của bạn đi?

practice.question: What about ___ some live acoustic music this weekend?

goal: Indicate that a singular person or animal owns something.
rule: Add an apostrophe and an 's' ('s) to the end of the singular noun.

examples.english: The head chef's personal knife is extremely sharp.
examples.vietnamese: Con dao cá nhân của vị bếp trưởng cực kỳ sắc bén.

practice.question: That software ___ premium laptop was left in the office.

goal: Indicate ownership when the possessor is a plural noun already ending in 's'.
rule: Add only an apostrophe (') after the existing plural 's'. Do not add an extra 's'.

examples.english: The factory workers' uniforms are made of durable cotton.
examples.vietnamese: Đồng phục của các công nhân nhà máy được làm bằng vải bông bền bỉ.

practice.question: The thirty ___ history textbooks were stacked on the desk.

topic: Object Pronouns: Singular review (Me / You / Him / Her / It)
goal: Review singular objective pronouns that receive a verb's action.
rule: Place object pronouns directly after transitive verbs or prepositions.

examples.english: The manager saw him fixing the critical system error.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý đã nhìn thấy anh ấy đang sửa lỗi hệ thống nghiêm trọng.

practice.question: I love this leather bag. My sister bought ___ for my birthday.

topic: Prepositions of Time: On + Specific Dates
goal: Anchor an action to a specific date or calendar day.
rule: Use 'on' before specific calendar dates (e.g., On March 15th) or days of the week.

examples.english: The new modern art gallery opened on October 20th.
examples.vietnamese: Triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại mới đã khai mạc vào ngày 20 tháng 10.

practice.question: The students will take their history exam ___ Monday morning.

topic: Prepositions of Time: In + Months / Years
goal: Anchor an action to a broader, non-specific period of time.
rule: Use 'in' before months, years, seasons, or centuries.

examples.english: He joined the software development company in 2024.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã gia nhập công ty phát triển phần mềm vào năm 2024.

practice.question: The weather in Da Nang is beautiful ___ July.

goal: Review singular pointers based on physical or temporal distance.
rule: Use 'this' for a singular item close to the speaker, and 'that' for an item further away.

examples.english: That painting over there is much older than this textbook.
examples.vietnamese: Bức tranh đằng kia cũ hơn nhiều so với cuốn sách giáo khoa này.

practice.question: ___ line of code right here on my screen contains an error.

goal: Review plural pointers based on physical or temporal distance.
rule: Use 'these' for plural items close to the speaker, and 'those' for plural items further away.

examples.english: Those leather bags on the top shelf are imported from Italy.
examples.vietnamese: Những chiếc túi da trên kệ trên cùng kia được nhập khẩu từ Ý.

practice.question: ___ fresh vegetables in my basket are for the traditional beef noodles.

goal: Categorize nouns correctly to apply proper verbs and articles.
rule: Countable nouns can be quantified with numbers and have plural forms. Uncountable nouns (mass, abstract, liquids) cannot be counted directly and take singular verbs.

examples.english: The factory manager bought three new machines and a lot of cash was saved.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý nhà máy đã mua ba chiếc máy mới và tiết kiệm được rất nhiều tiền mặt.

practice.question: The head chef needs more ___ to finish the traditional soup.

goal: Ask questions about past states or locations using was/were.
rule: Structure: Was/Were + subject + complement...? Do not use the auxiliary verb 'did'.

examples.english: Were you at the modern art gallery yesterday morning?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có ở triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại sáng qua không?

practice.question: ___ the software engineer at his desk when the system crashed?

topic: Past Simple: Wh- Questions with Did
goal: Inquire about details of past completed events using question words.
rule: Structure: Wh- Word + did + subject + base verb...?

examples.english: Where did you purchase that durable leather bag?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn đã mua chiếc túi da bền bỉ đó ở đâu vậy?

practice.question: When did the manager ___ the presentation about system architecture?

goal: Express general personal preferences towards objects or concepts.
rule: Follow the verb directly with a plural countable noun or an uncountable noun phrase to express general tastes.

examples.english: The apprentice loves traditional beef noodles but hates spicy food.
examples.vietnamese: Người học việc rất thích món phở bò truyền thống nhưng ghét đồ ăn cay.

practice.question: The thirty students absolutely love history ___.

goal: Modify nouns fundamentally using descriptive attributes.
rule: Place the base adjective directly BEFORE the noun it modifies, or after link verbs like 'be' or 'feel'. Adjectives do not have plural forms.

examples.english: The designer uses a premium laptop to draw modern artwork.
examples.vietnamese: Nhà thiết kế sử dụng một chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp để vẽ tác phẩm nghệ thuật hiện đại.

practice.question: The factory workers are handling some ___ glass panels.

topic: Verbs followed by Infinitives vs Gerunds: Meaning Change
goal: Differentiate meanings of verbs like 'remember', 'forget', or 'stop' based on the following verb form.
rule: Verb + gerund looks back at a past action. Verb + infinitive looks forward to an action or duty that needs to be done.

examples.english: He stopped playing video games to study. vs He stopped playing video games completely.
examples.vietnamese: Anh ấy đã dừng chơi game để học bài. vs Anh ấy đã thôi không chơi game nữa.

practice.question: Please remember ___ up the system code before leaving the office.

topic: Wish + Past Simple for Present Regrets
goal: Express a desire for a current situation to be different from reality.
rule: Structure: Subject + wish + subject + Past Simple. Use 'were' instead of 'was' for all subjects in formal contexts.

examples.english: I wish I had enough cash to buy that premium laptop right now.
examples.vietnamese: Ước gì tôi có đủ tiền mặt để mua chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp đó ngay bây giờ.

practice.question: The software engineer wishes the system code ___ fewer errors.

topic: Wish + Past Perfect for Past Regrets
goal: Express sadness or regret about an event that already happened in the past.
rule: Structure: Subject + wish + subject + Past Perfect (had + V3/V-ed).

examples.english: The designer wishes he had visited the modern art gallery in Da Nang last week.
examples.vietnamese: Nhà thiết kế ước gì anh ấy đã đến thăm triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại ở Đà Nẵng tuần trước.

practice.question: I wish I ___ taken the subway train instead of the regular bus yesterday.

goal: Focus on an action currently happening to an object rather than the person doing it.
rule: Structure: Subject + am/is/are + being + Past Participle (V3/V-ed).

examples.english: The traditional beef noodles are being prepared by the head chef right now.
examples.vietnamese: Món phở bò truyền thống hiện đang được chuẩn bị bởi bếp trưởng.

practice.question: The system code ___ tested for critical errors at this very moment.

goal: Describe an action that was in progress upon an object at a specific past point.
rule: Structure: Subject + was/were + being + Past Participle (V3/V-ed).

examples.english: The heavy factory machinery was being repaired when the manager walked in.
examples.vietnamese: Máy móc nhà máy hạng nặng đang được sửa chữa khi người quản lý bước vào.

practice.question: The thirty leather bags ___ packed into boxes when I arrived.

goal: Emphasize a completed action's effect on an object without focusing on the doer.
rule: Structure: Subject + has/have + been + Past Participle (V3/V-ed).

examples.english: The new modern art gallery has been visited by thousands of tourists this year.
examples.vietnamese: Triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại mới đã được ghé thăm bởi hàng ngàn du khách trong năm nay.

practice.question: The critical system error has finally ___ fixed by the engineer.

goal: Express necessity, ability, or advice regarding an object passive structure.
rule: Structure: Subject + modal verb + be + Past Participle (V3/V-ed).

examples.english: This premium laptop must be handled with absolute care.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp này phải được xử lý với sự cẩn thận tuyệt đối.

practice.question: The historical textbooks should ___ returned to the shelf before Friday.

goal: Combine multiple words into a single modifier for a noun.
rule: Join the words with a hyphen when they appear BEFORE the noun they modify. Do not pluralize the noun inside the modifier (e.g., a ten-page report).

examples.english: The company operates in a modern twenty-story building near the station.
examples.vietnamese: Công ty hoạt động trong một tòa nhà hai mươi tầng hiện đại gần nhà ga.

practice.question: The head chef prepared a ___ course meal for the special managers.

goal: Emphasize that the subject performed the action personally without help.
rule: Place the reflexive pronoun (myself, himself, itself, themselves) directly after the subject or at the end of the clause.

examples.english: The software engineer fixed the critical server code himself.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm đã tự mình sửa đoạn mã máy chủ nghiêm trọng.

practice.question: The designer ___ painted the entrance of the modern art gallery.

goal: Distinguish between reciprocal actions and actions performed independently on oneself.
rule: Use 'each other' for reciprocal actions between two or more parties. Use 'themselves' when the actors perform actions to their own bodies or minds.

examples.english: The two apprentice chefs helped each other prepare the beef noodles.
examples.vietnamese: Hai người đầu bếp học việc đã giúp đỡ lẫn nhau chuẩn bị món phở bò.

practice.question: The factory workers bought ___ new safety gloves to prevent injuries.

goal: Connect a clause that describes a physical location or place name smoothly.
rule: Replace 'preposition + which' (like in which, at which) with 'where' to introduce a defining relative clause about a place.

examples.english: This is the exact factory where they manufacture durable leather bags.
examples.vietnamese: Đây chính là nhà máy nơi họ sản xuất ra những chiếc túi da bền bỉ.

practice.question: Da Nang is the city ___ the designer built his modern art gallery.

goal: Connect a clause that describes a specific timeframe, day, or era smoothly.
rule: Use 'when' to introduce a relative clause modifying a noun of time (day, year, time, morning).

examples.english: 2024 was the year when the software engineer wrote his first system code.
examples.vietnamese: 2024 là năm khi kỹ sư phần mềm viết dòng mã hệ thống đầu tiên của mình.

practice.question: I remember the exact morning ___ the server crashed completely.

goal: Connect a clause explaining the reason behind a situation.
rule: Use 'why' almost exclusively after the noun 'the reason' to introduce an explanatory clause.

examples.english: The critical line error was the reason why the system failed the test.
examples.vietnamese: Lỗi dòng nghiêm trọng chính là lý do tại sao hệ thống thất bại trong bài kiểm tra.

practice.question: Do you know the reason ___ the manager canceled the team presentation?

topic: Indefinite Pronouns: Someone / Anyone / No one
goal: Refer to an unspecified person without naming them directly.
rule: Use 'someone' in affirmative statements, 'anyone' in questions and negatives, and 'no one' with positive verbs to convey a negative meaning. They always take singular verbs.

examples.english: Someone left a heavy history textbook inside the subway carriage.
examples.vietnamese: Ai đó đã để quên một cuốn sách giáo khoa lịch sử dày cộp bên trong toa tàu điện ngầm.

practice.question: Is there ___ in the office who knows how to fix this system code?

goal: Refer to an unspecified object or entity without pinpointing its exact identity.
rule: Apply the same affirmative/negative/question patterns as human indefinite pronouns. They take singular verbs.

examples.english: There is nothing inside this vintage leather bag.
examples.vietnamese: Không có gì bên trong chiếc túi da cổ điển này cả.

practice.question: The apprentice chef didn't say ___ to the strict head chef.

goal: Indicate ownership or association directly before a noun phrase.
rule: Place the possessive adjective directly before the noun. It must agree with the possessor, not the thing possessed.

examples.english: Our software development team successfully deployed the new system code.
examples.vietnamese: Đội ngũ phát triển phần mềm của chúng tôi đã triển khai thành công mã hệ thống mới.

practice.question: The head chef spilled some hot soup on ___ clean white uniform.

goal: Describe actions that happen repeatedly or regularly in everyday life.
rule: Use the base form of the verb for I/you/we/they. Add '-s' or '-es' for he/she/it.

examples.english: The regular bus arrives at the Da Nang transit station every morning at 7 AM.
examples.vietnamese: Xe buýt thông thường đến trạm trung chuyển Đà Nẵng mỗi sáng lúc 7 giờ.

practice.question: The factory manager ___ the system reports every single weekend.

goal: Indicate how often an action occurs using standard adverbs of frequency.
rule: Place frequency adverbs (always, usually, often, sometimes, never) BEFORE the main verb, but AFTER the 'be' verb.

examples.english: The apprentice is sometimes late because of the crowded subway train.
examples.vietnamese: Người học việc đôi khi bị trễ vì chuyến tàu điện ngầm quá đông đúc.

practice.question: The software engineer ___ backs up his critical source code.

goal: State permanent facts, scientific laws, or general realities.
rule: Use the present simple tense regardless of the current time frame.

examples.english: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius to make hot black coffee.
examples.vietnamese: Nước sôi ở 100 độ C để pha cà phê đen nóng.

practice.question: A premium laptop ___ more cash than a regular school textbook.

goal: Describe an ongoing state or action that is temporary, not a permanent routine.
rule: Structure: Subject + am/is/are + verb-ing combined with temporary time markers like 'this week' or 'these days'.

examples.english: The designer is staying in Da Nang this week to supervise the modern art gallery.
examples.vietnamese: Nhà thiết kế đang ở Đà Nẵng tuần này để giám sát triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại.

practice.question: The factory workers ___ extra hours this month to finish the leather bags.

goal: Talk about completed past actions using common irregular verbs.
rule: Do not add '-ed' to irregular verbs. Memorize their distinct past forms: go -> went, see -> saw, buy -> bought, have -> had.

examples.english: We saw a beautiful modern painting at the art gallery last weekend.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi đã nhìn thấy một bức tranh hiện đại tuyệt đẹp tại triển lãm nghệ thuật vào cuối tuần trước.

practice.question: The manager ___ a durable leather bag from the local shop yesterday.

goal: Form past tense sentences using structural or consumption irregular verbs.
rule: Use the specific V2 forms: make -> made, write -> wrote, take -> took, eat -> ate.

examples.english: The software engineer wrote thirty lines of code before the server crashed.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm đã viết ba mươi dòng mã trước khi máy chủ bị sập.

practice.question: The thirty students ___ the subway train to the historical museum.

topic: Prepositions of Place: Next to / Beside
goal: Indicate that an object is at the side of another object.
rule: Use 'next to' or 'beside' interchangeably. Do not include 'to' after 'beside'.

examples.english: The premium laptop is placed next to the heavy history textbook.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp được đặt cạnh cuốn sách giáo khoa lịch sử dày cộp.

practice.question: The apprentice sat ___ the head chef to learn the traditional recipe.

topic: Prepositions of Place: Near / Far from
goal: Describe spatial distance between two locations or objects fundamentally.
rule: Use 'near' without any following prepositions. Use 'far from' to describe a long distance.

examples.english: Our new corporate office is located very near the subway station.
examples.vietnamese: Văn phòng công ty mới của chúng tôi nằm rất gần ga tàu điện ngầm.

practice.question: The factory is far ___ the modern art gallery in Da Nang.

goal: Explain the cause or reason behind a situation within a single sentence.
rule: Place 'because' before the clause that contains the reason. No comma is required if it is in the middle of the sentence.

examples.english: The manager canceled the presentation because the system code had a critical error.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý đã hủy bài thuyết trình vì mã hệ thống gặp lỗi nghiêm trọng.

practice.question: He bought the leather bag ___ it was made of durable material.

goal: Form plural nouns correctly when the singular form ends in 'y'.
rule: If a noun ends in consonant + y, change 'y' to 'ies' (e.g., company -> companies). If it ends in vowel + y, just add 's' (e.g., day -> days).

examples.english: The tech firm operates three separate software development companies.
examples.vietnamese: Công ty công nghệ vận hành ba công ty phát triển phần mềm riêng biệt.

practice.question: There are many modern ___ in the city of Da Nang.

goal: Form plural nouns that require an extra syllable sound for pronunciation.
rule: Add '-es' to the end of nouns ending in -ch, -sh, -x, or -s (e.g., box -> boxes, bus -> buses).

examples.english: The factory workers loaded thirty wooden boxes onto the cargo train.
examples.vietnamese: Các công nhân nhà máy đã xếp ba mươi chiếc hộp gỗ lên tàu chở hàng.

practice.question: The regular subway ___ are usually crowded during the morning rush hour.

goal: Refer to a specific object that both the speaker and listener already know.
rule: Use 'the' before singular, plural, countable, or uncountable nouns when the identity is clear.

examples.english: The manager checked the code that the software engineer wrote yesterday.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý đã kiểm tra đoạn mã mà kỹ sư phần mềm viết ngày hôm qua.

practice.question: Could you pass me ___ leather bag on the desk over there?

topic: Articles: Zero Article for General Plurals
goal: Talk about things or people in general without targeting specific ones.
rule: Do not use 'a', 'an', or 'the' when making generalized statements with plural countable nouns.

examples.english: Durable leather bags are usually more expensive than plastic ones.
examples.vietnamese: Túi da bền bỉ thường đắt hơn túi nhựa.

practice.question: ___ software engineers usually drink black coffee to stay awake.

goal: Ask about actions or events currently taking place right now.
rule: Structure: Am/Is/Are + subject + verb-ing...?

examples.english: Are the thirty students listening to live acoustic music right now?
examples.vietnamese: Có phải ba mươi học sinh đang nghe nhạc acoustic sống ngay lúc này không?

practice.question: Why ___ the head chef preparing traditional beef noodles so early?

goal: Refer to undefined spatial locations or geographical positions smoothly.
rule: Follow the same affirmative/negative clauses rules as other indefinite pronouns. 'Nowhere' creates a negative sentence and takes an affirmative verb.

examples.english: The designer searched everywhere for his premium drawing laptop.
examples.vietnamese: Nhà thiết kế đã tìm kiếm khắp mọi nơi chiếc máy tính xách tay vẽ cao cấp của mình.

practice.question: There is ___ quite like the local art gallery in Da Nang.

goal: Express general rules, institutional regulations, or automated factory logic.
rule: Structure: If/When + Present Simple, Present Simple. Both parts state standard, unchangeable facts.

examples.english: If the factory workers press the red button, the machinery stops immediately.
examples.vietnamese: Nếu công nhân nhà máy nhấn nút màu đỏ, máy móc sẽ dừng lại ngay lập tức.

practice.question: When the system code contains a critical error, the server ___ automatically.

goal: Use multi-word verbs where the noun object can sit between the verb and particle.
rule: If the object is a pronoun (it, them), it MUST go in the middle. If it is a noun, it can go in the middle or at the end.

examples.english: The software engineer turned the system off. / He turned it off.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm đã tắt hệ thống. / Anh ấy đã tắt nó.

practice.question: The student had a heavy history textbook and put ___ inside his bag.

goal: Use multi-word verbs where the object must always follow the full phrase.
rule: The object (noun or pronoun) cannot break the verb and its particle apart (e.g., look after, look for).

examples.english: The manager is looking for his durable leather bag everywhere.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý đang tìm kiếm chiếc túi da bền bỉ của mình ở khắp mọi nơi.

practice.question: The apprentice chef must look ___ the strict head chef's instructions.

topic: Quantifiers: A great deal of / A large amount of
goal: Express large quantities formally with uncountable mass concepts.
rule: Use 'a great deal of' or 'a large amount of' exclusively before uncountable nouns like cash, time, energy.

examples.english: Building a modern art gallery in Da Nang requires a great deal of cash.
examples.vietnamese: Xây dựng một triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại ở Đà Nẵng đòi hỏi một lượng tiền mặt rất lớn.

practice.question: The development team spent a large amount of ___ testing the system code.

topic: Quantifiers: A wide variety of / A large number of
goal: Express massive diversity or quantity specifically with plural countable nouns.
rule: Follow these phrases with a plural countable noun. They take a plural verb form.

examples.english: A wide variety of premium leather bags are manufactured in this factory.
examples.vietnamese: Rất nhiều loại túi da cao cấp đa dạng được sản xuất tại nhà máy này.

practice.question: A large number of ___ visited the historical museum yesterday.

topic: Adverbs of Degree: Quite / Rather / Pretty
goal: Modify the strength of adjectives moderately or slightly.
rule: Place these adverbs directly before adjectives. 'Rather' can imply a negative or surprising quality, while 'pretty' and 'quite' are standard modifiers.

examples.english: The history exam was rather difficult, but the thirty students all passed.
examples.vietnamese: Kỳ thi lịch sử khá là khó, nhưng ba mươi học sinh đều đã vượt qua.

practice.question: The traditional beef noodles at this local shop are ___ good.

topic: Future Plans: Present Continuous vs Be going to
goal: Differentiate between fixed social arrangements and general future intentions.
rule: Use Present Continuous for pre-arranged events with a fixed time/place (e.g., meeting a doctor). Use 'be going to' for general personal intents or plans without fixed external confirmation.

examples.english: The designer is meeting the corporate manager at the art gallery at 3 PM tomorrow.
examples.vietnamese: Nhà thiết kế sẽ gặp người quản lý công ty tại triển lãm nghệ thuật vào lúc 3 giờ chiều mai (đã hẹn).

practice.question: I ___ buy a durable rubber raincoat, but I haven't decided which shop yet.

topic: Time Clauses: As soon as / Once in Future contexts
goal: Link a primary future action to the immediate completion of a secondary event.
rule: Use the Present Simple tense immediately inside the time clause (after as soon as/once), and the Future Simple in the main clause.

examples.english: As soon as the software engineer fixes the line of code, the system will run smoothly.
examples.vietnamese: Ngay khi kỹ sư phần mềm sửa xong dòng mã, hệ thống sẽ chạy mượt mà.

practice.question: Once the head chef ___ the ingredients, the apprentice will start the fire.

topic: Relative Clauses: Omission of Object Relative Pronouns
goal: Streamline defining relative clauses by dropping unnecessary relative pronouns.
rule: You can completely omit 'who', 'which', or 'that' ONLY when the relative pronoun acts as the object of the relative clause, followed by another subject.

examples.english: The leather bag (which) I bought last week is incredibly durable.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc túi da (mà) tôi mua tuần trước vô cùng bền bỉ.

practice.question: The premium laptop ___ the engineer used to write code belongs to the company.

topic: Past Continuous: Interrupted Actions with While / When
goal: Describe a longer ongoing past action that was interrupted by a shorter, sudden event.
rule: Use Past Continuous for the longer background action (usually with while), and Past Simple for the interrupting action (usually with when).

examples.english: While the factory workers were packing the leather bags, the power went out.
examples.vietnamese: Trong khi các công nhân nhà máy đang đóng gói túi da thì điện bị mất.

practice.question: The software engineer was writing system code when the server ___ down.

goal: Express excessive and problematic quantities of specific items.
rule: Use 'too much' with uncountable nouns and 'too many' with plural countable nouns.

examples.english: The software engineer drank too much black coffee and couldn't sleep.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm đã uống quá nhiều cà phê đen và không thể ngủ được.

practice.question: There are too ___ critical errors inside this new line of code.

goal: Indicate actions completed very recently or earlier than expected.
rule: Place 'just' or 'already' between the auxiliary verb 'have/has' and the past participle (V3/V-ed).

examples.english: The head chef has just finished cooking the traditional beef noodles.
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng vừa mới nấu xong món phở bò truyền thống.

practice.question: The thirty students have ___ bought their history textbooks.

goal: Inquire about or state an expected action that has not occurred up to the current moment.
rule: Place 'yet' at the absolute end of negative sentences or questions in the Present Perfect tense.

examples.english: The designer hasn't completed the modern art gallery blueprints yet.
examples.vietnamese: Nhà thiết kế vẫn chưa hoàn thành bản thiết kế triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại.

practice.question: Has the software engineer deployed the system code ___?

goal: Show formal contrast or cause-and-effect transitions across sentences.
rule: Start a new sentence with 'However' or 'Therefore', immediately followed by a comma, to link back to the previous sentence's concept.

examples.english: The premium laptop was extremely expensive. However, the designer still purchased it.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp cực kỳ đắt tiền. Tuy nhiên, nhà thiết kế vẫn quyết định mua nó.

practice.question: The server crashed completely. ___ , the corporate presentation was delayed.

goal: State that an action is not happening at the current moment.
rule: Structure: Subject + am/is/are + not + verb-ing. Contracted forms (isn't, aren't) are common.

examples.english: The software engineer isn't working on the server code right now.
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm không có đang làm việc với mã máy chủ ngay lúc này.

practice.question: The factory workers ___ packing the durable leather bags at this moment.

topic: Past Simple: Negative Form with Regular Verbs
goal: Deny that an action occurred in the past using regular base verbs.
rule: Structure: Subject + did not (didn't) + base verb. Do not add '-ed' to the main verb after 'didn't'.

examples.english: The thirty students didn't visit the historical museum last week.
examples.vietnamese: Ba mươi học sinh đã không đến thăm bảo tàng lịch sử vào tuần trước.

practice.question: The manager didn't ___ the new system presentation yesterday morning.

topic: Past Simple: Negative Form with Irregular Verbs
goal: Deny past irregular actions properly without shifting the main verb.
rule: Structure: Subject + did not (didn't) + base verb (bare form). Never use the V2 form after 'didn't'.

examples.english: We didn't buy any premium leather bags during our trip to Da Nang.
examples.vietnamese: Chúng tôi đã không mua chiếc túi da cao cấp nào trong chuyến đi Đà Nẵng cả.

practice.question: The head chef didn't ___ to the office to meet the corporate team.

topic: Prepositions of Place: Opposite / In front of comparison
goal: Distinguish between facing structures versus linear positions.
rule: Use 'opposite' when two entities are facing each other (usually with a space or street between). Use 'in front of' for being further forward than something.

examples.english: The modern art gallery is located directly opposite the subway station.
examples.vietnamese: Triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại nằm đối diện ngay ga tàu điện ngầm.

practice.question: The regular bus stopped right in ___ the main factory gate.

goal: Describe an object positioned at the back or rear of another entity.
rule: Use 'behind' directly before the noun phrase without adding extra prepositions like 'of'.

examples.english: The office cat is hiding behind the heavy history textbooks.
examples.vietnamese: Con mèo văn phòng đang trốn phía sau những cuốn sách giáo khoa lịch sử dày cộp.

practice.question: He parked his new motorcycle ___ the corporate building.

topic: Possessive Pronouns: Introduction (Mine / Yours / His / Hers)
goal: Replace a possessive adjective + noun combination to avoid repetition.
rule: Structure: Subject/Object position. Mine = my + noun; Yours = your + noun, etc. Do not place a noun after a possessive pronoun.

examples.english: This premium laptop is mine, and that durable leather bag is hers.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp này là của tôi, và chiếc túi da bền bỉ kia là của cô ấy.

practice.question: I found a history textbook on the bus. Is it ___?

goal: Replace plural possessive phrases smoothly within a conversation.
rule: Ours = our + noun; Theirs = their + noun. These forms already contain the noun concept.

examples.english: The development team said the successful system code was ours.
examples.vietnamese: Đội ngũ phát triển nói rằng mã hệ thống thành công đó là của chúng tôi.

practice.question: The factory workers claimed that those wooden boxes were ___.

topic: Adverbs of Frequency: Adverbial Phrases (Once a week / Every day)
goal: Express exact frequency intervals using multi-word time phrases.
rule: Place phrases like 'every day', 'once a week', 'twice a month', or 'three times a year' at the absolute end of the sentence.

examples.english: The head chef cooks traditional beef noodles for his family once a week.
examples.vietnamese: Bếp trưởng nấu phở bò truyền thống cho gia đình anh ấy mỗi tuần một lần.

practice.question: The software engineer tests the system stability ___ day.

goal: Ask a direct question to discover the owner of an object.
rule: Structure: Whose + noun + is this/are those...? OR Whose + is this/are those + noun...?

examples.english: Whose premium laptop is left running in the conference room?
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp của ai đang để chạy trong phòng hội nghị thế?

practice.question: ___ leather bag is that on the head chef's desk?

goal: State that something is fundamentally impossible based on clear immediate facts.
rule: Use 'cannot' or 'can't' to express a strong feeling that a situation is impossible or untrue.

examples.english: This can't be the correct line of code; it has thirty structural errors.
examples.vietnamese: Đây không thể nào là dòng mã đúng được; nó có tới ba mươi lỗi cấu trúc.

practice.question: He ___ be the head chef; he doesn't know how to cook traditional noodles.

topic: Prepositions of Time: At + Exact Clock Times
goal: Pinpoint an event to an exact specific hour or chronological point.
rule: Use 'at' before precise times of day (e.g., at 8:30 AM), holiday periods (at Christmas), and specific expressions (at night, at the weekend).

examples.english: The factory workers start their shifts at exactly 8 AM.
examples.vietnamese: Các công nhân nhà máy bắt đầu ca làm việc của họ vào đúng 8 giờ sáng.

practice.question: The subway train arrives ___ noon to pick up the museum tourists.

topic: Prepositions of Time: From... To... intervals
goal: Define a clear start and end point for a time duration.
rule: Structure: from [start time] + to/until + [end time]. Applicable to hours, days, months, or years.

examples.english: The modern art gallery is open from Tuesday to Sunday every week.
examples.vietnamese: Triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại mở cửa từ thứ Ba đến chủ nhật hàng tuần.

practice.question: The software team analyzed the system errors from 9 AM ___ 4 PM.

topic: Quantifiers: Any in Negatives and Questions
goal: State zero quantity or inquire about the existence of plural/mass items.
rule: Use 'any' with plural countable nouns and uncountable nouns in negative sentences and regular questions.

examples.english: The apprentice doesn't have any cash left inside his leather bag.
examples.vietnamese: Người học việc không còn một đồng tiền mặt nào bên trong chiếc túi da của mình.

practice.question: Are there ___ system errors in the code written by the manager?

goal: Use 'some' exceptionally in question formats when expecting a positive reply.
rule: Use 'some' (instead of any) in questions when offering something or making a polite request.

examples.english: Would you like some hot traditional beef noodles for lunch?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn có muốn dùng một chút phở bò truyền thống nóng hổi cho bữa trưa không?

practice.question: Could you please pass me ___ clean water from the counter?

goal: Review plural objective pronouns serving as objects in team environments.
rule: Place 'us' or 'you' after main action verbs or location/direction prepositions.

examples.english: The corporate manager invited us to visit the modern art gallery in Da Nang.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý công ty đã mời chúng tôi đến thăm triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại ở Đà Nẵng.

practice.question: The factory workers are waiting for the bus. Can you see ___?

goal: Express completely hypothetical scenarios about past events that did not happen.
rule: Structure: If + Past Perfect, Subject + would have + Past Participle (V3/V-ed). Reflects on past unchangeable choices.

examples.english: If the software engineer had backed up the code, the server wouldn't have lost the system data.
examples.vietnamese: Nếu kỹ sư phần mềm sao lưu mã nguồn, máy chủ đã không bị mất dữ liệu hệ thống.

practice.question: If you had checked the map, we would ___ arrived in Da Nang on time.

goal: Focus on the future outcome happening to an object rather than who does it.
rule: Structure: Subject + will be + Past Participle (V3/V-ed).

examples.english: The thirty leather bags will be manufactured by the factory next month.
examples.vietnamese: Ba mươi chiếc túi da sẽ được sản xuất bởi nhà máy vào tháng tới.

practice.question: The new lines of code will ___ tested by the development team tomorrow.

goal: Provide non-essential extra location descriptions surrounded by commas.
rule: Use 'where' after a proper noun location or specific place name. Separate the clause using commas.

examples.english: Da Nang, where the designer built his modern art gallery, is a beautiful coastal city.
examples.vietnamese: Đà Nẵng, nơi nhà thiết kế xây dựng triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại, là một thành phố biển xinh đẹp.

practice.question: The main corporate office, ___ the system server is kept, is heavily guarded.

goal: Master complex phrasal verbs that contain both an adverbial particle and a preposition.
rule: These verbs are always inseparable (e.g., look forward to, run out of, get along with, keep up with).

examples.english: The development team unfortunately ran out of time during the presentation.
examples.vietnamese: Đội ngũ phát triển không may đã hết thời gian trong suốt bài thuyết trình.

practice.question: The thirty students are looking forward ___ visiting the historical museum.

topic: Infinitives: Adverbial modifier with Too / Enough
goal: Link an adjective modifier directly to a resulting capability or restriction using an infinitive.
rule: Structure: too + adjective + to-infinitive OR adjective + enough + to-infinitive.

examples.english: This premium laptop is too expensive for the apprentice to buy.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp này quá đắt đối với người học việc để có thể mua.

practice.question: The factory worker was strong enough ___ carry the thirty wooden boxes.

topic: Verbs of Perception + Base Verb vs Gerund
goal: Differentiate between witnessing a full completed action versus a partial ongoing action.
rule: Verb of perception (see, hear, watch) + object + base verb means seeing the whole event. + verb-ing means seeing an action in progress.

examples.english: I watched the head chef cook the entire meal. vs I saw him walking down the street.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi đã xem bếp trưởng nấu toàn bộ bữa ăn. vs Tôi nhìn thấy anh ấy đang đi bộ trên đường.

practice.question: The manager heard the software engineer ___ about the system error.

topic: Question Tags: Present and Past Tense review
goal: Seek confirmation or agreement at the end of a statement.
rule: If the main statement is positive, the tag is negative. If the statement is negative, the tag is positive. Match the auxiliary verb tense.

examples.english: The software engineer fixed the system code, didn't he?
examples.vietnamese: Kỹ sư phần mềm đã sửa mã hệ thống rồi, phải không anh ấy?

practice.question: Those traditional beef noodles aren't spicy, ___ they?

goal: Give an urgent warning or recommendation where negative consequences exist.
rule: Structure: Subject + had better ('d better) + base verb (bare infinitive). Negative form: had better not.

examples.english: You had better back up that system code before the server reboots.
examples.vietnamese: Bạn tốt hơn hết nên sao lưu đoạn mã hệ thống đó trước khi máy chủ khởi động lại.

practice.question: The apprentice had better ___ waste his cash on low-quality leather material.

goal: Quantify the duration required for an individual to complete a task.
rule: Structure: It takes/took + person object + time duration + to-infinitive.

examples.english: It took the factory workers thirty days to finish the luxury leather bags.
examples.vietnamese: Phải mất ba mươi ngày các công nhân nhà máy mới hoàn thành số túi da sang trọng.

practice.question: It takes the head chef three hours ___ the traditional beef broth.

goal: Embed a yes/no option or doubt as a conceptual noun clause within a statement.
rule: Structure: Subject + verb (wonder/don't know) + whether + subject + verb. Often paired with 'or not'.

examples.english: The manager wonders whether the software engineer has completed the code test.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý băn khoăn liệu kỹ sư phần mềm đã hoàn thành bài kiểm tra mã nguồn chưa.

practice.question: I don't know ___ the modern art gallery opens on Monday mornings or not.

goal: Shorten a sentence when one subject performs two actions at the same time.
rule: Replace the secondary clause with a verb-ing phrase directly to streamline flow.

examples.english: Walking into the modern art gallery, the designer saw many beautiful paintings.
examples.vietnamese: Bước vào triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại, nhà thiết kế đã nhìn thấy nhiều bức tranh đẹp.

practice.question: ___ the system manual carefully, the engineer fixed the critical error.

topic: Double Comparatives: The more... the more...
goal: Show that two changing situations are directly dependent on each other.
rule: Structure: The + comparative adjective/adverb + clause, the + comparative adjective/adverb + clause.

examples.english: The more difficult the system code is, the more focused the engineer becomes.
examples.vietnamese: Mã hệ thống càng khó, người kỹ sư lại càng trở nên tập trung hơn.

practice.question: The older the modern art gallery painting is, ___ valuable it becomes.

topic: Prepositions of Cause: Due to / Owing to
goal: Link a formal negative or neutral outcome to its causing noun phrase.
rule: Follow 'due to' or 'owing to' with a noun phrase, pronoun, or gerund. They function similarly to 'because of'.

examples.english: The presentation delay was due to a critical system error on the server.
examples.vietnamese: Sự chậm trễ của bài thuyết trình là do lỗi hệ thống nghiêm trọng trên máy chủ.

practice.question: Owing ___ the heavy rain, the students postponed their trip to the museum.

goal: Explain doing an action now to prepare for a possible future event or problem.
rule: Structure: Main Clause + in case + Present Simple clause. Do not use 'will' inside the in case clause.

examples.english: The factory worker carried a durable rubber raincoat in case it rained heavily.
examples.vietnamese: Người công nhân nhà máy mang theo một chiếc áo mưa cao su bền bỉ phòng trường hợp trời mưa to.

practice.question: Please bring extra cash in case the local shop ___ credit cards.

topic: Adverbs of Manner: Irregular review (Lately / Hardly warning)
goal: Understand that 'lately' and 'hardly' are not manners, but time/degree modifiers.
rule: Warning: 'Hardly' means almost not at all (not the adverb of hard). 'Lately' means recently (not the adverb of late). Keep 'hard' and 'late' as their own adverbs.

examples.english: The development team has worked extremely hard lately to fix the server code.
examples.vietnamese: Đội ngũ phát triển gần đây đã làm việc vô cùng chăm chỉ để sửa mã máy chủ.

practice.question: The software engineer could ___ sleep due to the stress of the critical error.

goal: Ask questions to express surprise or seek confirmation about a negative state.
rule: Structure: Don't / Doesn't + subject + base verb...? Keep the main verb in its bare form.

examples.english: Don't you want to eat traditional beef noodles at this local restaurant?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn không muốn ăn phở bò truyền thống ở nhà hàng địa phương này sao?

practice.question: ___ the software engineer know how to fix this critical system error?

topic: Past Simple: Short Answers (Yes, I did / No, I didn't)
goal: Respond to past tense closed questions quickly and naturally.
rule: Use 'did' for positive short answers and 'didn't' for negative short answers, matching the subject pronoun.

examples.english: Did the manager approve the new presentation? Yes, he did.
examples.vietnamese: Người quản lý đã phê duyệt bài thuyết trình mới chưa? Vâng, anh ấy đã làm vậy.

practice.question: Did the thirty students enjoy the history textbook? No, they ___.

topic: Prepositions of Place: Under / Above comparison
goal: Describe vertical positions relative to a surface or object without direct contact.
rule: Use 'above' for a position higher than something, and 'under' for a position directly below or covered by something.

examples.english: The modern painting was hung directly above the leather bag display.
examples.vietnamese: Bức tranh hiện đại được treo ngay phía trên khu trưng bày túi da.

practice.question: The office cat slept comfortably ___ the manager's desk.

topic: Subject Pronouns vs Object Pronouns full review
goal: Verify the correct placement of pronouns based on their syntactic role.
rule: Subject pronouns (I, we, they) initiate actions before the verb. Object pronouns (me, us, them) receive actions after the verb or preposition.

examples.english: They helped us carry thirty wooden boxes into the main factory building.
examples.vietnamese: Họ đã giúp chúng tôi khiêng ba mươi chiếc hộp gỗ vào tòa nhà nhà máy chính.

practice.question: The designer designed a premium laptop case, and the manager praised ___.

goal: Select the correct quantifier based on noun countability classifications.
rule: Use 'many' strictly with plural countable nouns. Use 'much' strictly with uncountable nouns (primarily in negatives and questions).

examples.english: How much cash do you have left inside your durable leather bag?
examples.vietnamese: Bạn còn lại bao nhiêu tiền mặt bên trong chiếc túi da bền bỉ của mình?

practice.question: There aren't ___ software engineers working on the system code today.

goal: Describe how an action is performed using standard regular adverbs.
rule: Add '-ly' to base descriptive adjectives (e.g., careful -> carefully, slow -> slowly). Place them after the verb or object.

examples.english: The apprentice chef chopped the fresh vegetables very slowly.
examples.vietnamese: Người đầu bếp học việc đã băm số rau củ tươi rất chậm rãi.

practice.question: Please review this critical line of code ___ before deployment.

goal: Master common plural nouns that do not employ the standard '-s' suffix.
rule: Memorize distinct mutations: man -> men, woman -> women, child -> children, person -> people.

examples.english: Thirty people queued outside the modern art gallery in Da Nang.
examples.vietnamese: Ba mươi người đã xếp hàng bên ngoài triển lãm nghệ thuật hiện đại ở Đà Nẵng.

practice.question: The factory manager hired five new ___ to operate the heavy machinery.

goal: Connect two opposing or contrasting ideas within a single sentence structure.
rule: Place 'but' between two independent clauses, typically preceded by a comma.

examples.english: The premium laptop is incredibly fast, but it costs too much cash.
examples.vietnamese: Chiếc máy tính xách tay cao cấp cực kỳ nhanh, nhưng nó tốn quá nhiều tiền mặt.

practice.question: The student read the history textbook, ___ he didn't pass the exam.

topic: Prepositions of Place: At for Specific Points
goal: Identify a specific target spot, institutional building, or event address.
rule: Use 'at' for precise locations (at the door), full addresses, or public locations (at the bus stop, at the station).

examples.english: The development team is meeting the client at the main office entrance.
examples.vietnamese: Đội ngũ phát triển đang gặp khách hàng tại lối vào văn phòng chính.

practice.question: We waited for thirty minutes ___ the subway transit station.

topic: Time Expressions: Past Time Markers (Ago / Last / Yesterday)
goal: Anchor a completed past simple statement to a fixed past duration or unit.
